But, as ever, her family contrives to take the gloss off her moment of glory. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Richard Skinner introduce performances by Gary Moore and Phil Lynott, Kool and the Gang, Propaganda, David Bowie, Duran Duran and Paul Hardcastle. First aired June 6 1985

programme.title: Top of the Pops

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: A profile of singer and pianist Ray Charles. Featuring the reclusive star in conversation with Charlie Gillett and clips from his 1986 Royal Festival Hall concert and earlier performances

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Hurricanes and Heatwaves: The Highs and Lows of British Weather
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the evolution of the weather forecast, from print via radio to TV and beyond, featuring an insight into how the Met Office and the BBC have always used the latest technology to bring the holy grail of accurate forecasting that much closer. Yet, as hand-drawn maps have been replaced by weather apps, the bigger drama of global warming has been playing itself out, as if to prove that people were right all along to obsess about the weather

programme.title: Heatwave
programme.desc: Bernard Hill narates a dramatised documentary of a fictionalised scenario that sees Britain caught in the grip of the hottest summer on record and caught in the grip of an environmental and social nightmare, set in the year 2006. The transport system is grinding to a halt, hospitals are overcrowded and large parts of the countryside are ablaze. First broadcast in 2005

programme.title: 1976: The Summer of - Heat
programme.desc: Recollections of the summer when the country suffered the longest dry spell on record, which led to drought, water rationing and forest fires. In the same year, James Hunt won the British Grand Prix, riots marred the Notting Hill Carnival, and punk music was born

programme.title: The World About Us
programme.desc: Barry Paine narrates a programme looking at the crisis facing British wildlife during the summer of 1976, the driest for two centuries, asking why there were so many ladybirds. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: A 1976 edition examining the UK's worst drought for over 200 years, revealing the British Isles were smack in the middle of a meteorological freak that began in the early 1970s. The programme looks at how water shortages would affect crops and food supplies and how best to conserve supplies. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Michael's journey progresses from the author's birthplace in the American Midwest to northern Michigan, where the intrepid adventurer spends a few hours trying his hand at fishing and shooting as Hemingway did as a child. He then follows the spirited young man's path across the Atlantic into Europe. First broadcast in 1999

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: The Modern Age of the Coach
programme.desc: Timeshift documentary exploring Britons' love of coach journeys in recent years, from school trips to outings for football fans and band tours. The programme looks at efforts to maintain interest in the mode of transport, including alternatives to the National Coach Company's standardised offerings, introduced in the 1970s. Narrated by Julian Rhind-Tutt

programme.title: Michael Palin Remembers - Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: The much-loved broadcaster, writer and traveller reflects on his journey through the landscapes, cultures and stories that shaped the work of Ernest Hemingway. From the author's homes and favourite haunts to the adventures that inspired his greatest works, Michael shares the highlights of his travels, revealing what he learned about the man behind the legend

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: The Monty Python star follows in the footsteps of one of his favourite writers, retracing Ernest Hemingway's journeys across Europe, Africa and America. Michael begins his odyssey in Spain, where he explores bullfighting in Valencia, before heading for Africa to see the spectacular Mount Kilimanjaro. First broadcast in 1999

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Michael's journey progresses from the author's birthplace in the American Midwest to northern Michigan, where the intrepid adventurer spends a few hours trying his hand at fishing and shooting as Hemingway did as a child. He then follows the spirited young man's path across the Atlantic into Europe. First broadcast in 1999

programme.title: Windrush: Portraits of a Generation
programme.desc: Marking the 75th anniversary of the arrival of the HMT Empire Windrush to British shores in 1948, cameras follow a project spearheaded by King Charles. He commissioned 10 leading artists, from Britain and abroad, to create portraits of 10 pioneering members of the Windrush Generation. The sitters, many in their nineties, speak frankly about their experiences of arriving in Britain, making lives for themselves, and the contribution they have made to their communities and the nation

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: USA v Paraguay (Kick-off 2.00am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the Group D match at Los Angeles Stadium in Inglewood. While the Americans were guaranteed a place in this tournament as one of the three co-hosts, Paraguay progressed from the CONMEBOL qualification process, and though they finished sixth in the standings, they were one of four teams to finish tied on 28 points and were just one point behind second-placed Ecuador. There have been nine previous meetings between these nations, the first of which saw the USA claim a 3-0 victory at the inaugural World Cup in Uruguay in 1930, and most recently they clashed in a friendly encounter in November last year, when the United States triumphed by a 2-1 scoreline. With analysis from Ashley Williams and Steph Houghton

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The HSBC Championships. Live coverage of day five of the women's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring the singles semi-finals. Top seed Zheng Qinwen was eliminated by Amanda Anisimova at this stage last year, with the eventual Wimbledon runner-up prevailing 6-2, 4-6, 6-4. There was an upset inn the other contest, with 2025 Australian Open champion Madison Keys losing 6-3, 7-6 to qualifier Tatjana Maria

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Brazil v Morocco (Kick-off 11.00pm). Kelly Cates presents coverage of the Group C match at New York/New Jersey Stadium in the USA. Brazil are perennial competitors at the World Cup, having appeared in all 22 previous stagings of the tournament, while their five victories in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002 are more than any other nation has achieved. However, they are far from the dominant force they once were and finished fifth in the CONMEBOL qualification with a record of eight wins, four draws and six losses from their 18 games. In contrast, Morocco finished with an unblemished record in their qualifying campaign, winning all eight matches, while scoring 22 goals and conceding just two. They come into this tournament on the back of a controversial appearance in the AFCON final just five months ago, when they lost 1-0 to Senegal in extra time after Senegal left the pitch and refused to return after conceding a penalty in the final minute of regulation time, which Morocco's Brahim Diaz then missed when the game eventually resumed. However, three months later the CAF Appeal Board ruled that Senegal had forfeited the final through their actions, awarding Morocco a 3-0 victory, subject to a ruling from the Court of Arbitration for Sport. With analysis from Joe Hart and Hassan Kachloul

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Haiti v Scotland (Kick-off 2.00am). Kelly Cates presents coverage of the Group C match at Boston Stadium in the USA. Scotland are appearing at a World Cup finals for the first time since France '98, and their eighth overall, having finished top of a qualifying group that contained Denmark, Greece and Belarus. This will be their first-ever meeting with Haiti, who had to come through two phases of qualification to reach the finals for the second time in their history, with the previous appearance being in 1974, in West Germany. They will hope to perform better than they did on that occasion, when they lost three from three and conceded 14 goals, so will welcome an opener against an opponent that has only won four World Cup finals matches in their history, the most recent of which was a 2-1 win over Sweden at Italia '90. With analysis from Joe Hart, Scott Brown and Rachel Corsie

programme.desc: John Craven and Vick Hope are in the Cotswolds, meeting people helping to support nature and discovering the skills and craftsmanship preserving the region's distinctive character. Meanwhile, Adam Henson gets hands-on with some of the region's rare breeds and the efforts to secure their future

programme.title: Tiger Island
programme.desc: Part two of two. As monsoon storms loom and temperatures soar, the big cats become more active at night. Tiger mother Goma teaches her cubs to become independent, while the arrival of a huge dominant male presents a rare opportunity to study tiger family dynamics. The crew must adapt to the challenging conditions, deploying military-grade thermal cameras and drones

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Thirlestane Castle in the Scottish Borders, where treasures include an art nouveau claret jug, a life-size leather tiger and an ice axe signed by famous mountaineers. Fiona also looks into the short but stellar career of Scottish racing legend Jim Clark and gets to see some of his personal items and trophies

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Janie runs a course for rule-breaking officers, including Clarke's old boss. When he is found dead in a toilet, Janie is forced to lock down CID to find the killer. While focused on the case and suspects who by their very nature are dishonest, Janie struggles to find time for her renewed relationship with her dad

programme.title: Outrageous
programme.desc: Drama following the six aristocratic Mitford sisters as they come of age during the turbulent 1930s, starring Bessie Carter and based on Mary Lovell's biography, The Mitford Girls. It's 1931 and, while Nancy lives in vain hope that her boyfriend Hamish will propose, her younger sister Diana has it all

programme.title: MOTD Live: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Ivory Coast v Ecuador (Kick-off 12.00am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the Group E match at Philadelphia Stadium in the USA. This will be the first ever meeting between these nations, and both will be aiming to get their campaigns off to a winning start having performed admirably in qualifying for this tournament. Les Éléphants finished atop their group in the CAF region after finishing with a record of eight wins and two draws from their 10 matches, while La Tricolor finished second in the highly competitive CONMEBOL region, trailing only Argentina, even after they were deducted three points related to an infringement from a previous World Cup qualification cycle. Neither of these nations has a stellar World Cup record from previous appearances, with Ivory Coast never having progressed beyond the group stage, and not having qualified since 2014, while Ecuador have appeared at four World Cup finals, most recently four years ago, making it to the last-16 on just one occasion. With analysis from Benni McCarthy and Ellen White

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton and Michelle Ackerley host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Gemma and Lorcan throw a party, but Gemma finds one of her old personality quirks coming to the fore again as she struggles to maintain her identity amidst the intoxicating brew of Lorcan's old-fashioned country charm. Callum makes a new friend in Gemma's brother David, but finds there's a catch. Comedy, with Rian Lennon, Ryan Dylan and Ele McKenzie

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna tries to find a boyfriend to please her mother in the first episode of the comedy series following the lives and loves of five young adults from Runcorn. With Sheridan Smith, Ralf Little, Beverley Callard, Will Mellor and Natalie Casey

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Louise starts losing weight and gets stretch marks, Jonny decides to stop being Mr Nice Guy, and Gaz gets cold feet over his relationship with Donna. Comedy, starring Ralf Little and Will Mellor

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy is hopeful that he could be fighting again soon, and he and Molly-Mae are spending more time together. Then, disaster strikes when KSI cancels the night when Roman was due to be in the fighting, claiming illness. John is furious, but decides the boys need cheering up so tells them he is taking them on a camping trip. However, neither Tommy nor Roman share their father's love of the outdoors

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy heads to Budapest to close the deal for the fight, but there's a hitch. With just four weeks to go, John calls the deal off because the money hasn't reached Tommy's account

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: It's Tommy's birthday, but there's no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction - juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving he's still got what it takes in the ring

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters search for the world's best four-seat supercar, pitting the Aston Martin Rapide against the Porsche Panamera Turbo and Maserati Quattroporte in a series of unusual challenges in the south of England. Harry Potter star Rupert Grint and ex-Formula One driver Rubens Barrichello each do a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car and Richard Hammond puts the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG through their paces on the test track

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Dane Buckley hosts the contest at Mandela Hall, Belfast, where five comics compete in the last of six knockout heats to bag their place in the grand final. Award-winning comedian and podcaster Alison Spittle and former NCA finalist William Thompson join head judge Fatiha El-Ghorri to assess the potential of contenders Eoghan Collins, Pravanya Pillay, Vinny Shiu, Lianna Holston and Jim Midge

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. Clara's life is devastated by a sudden tragedy, and she seeks the Doctor's help in putting things right. This personal mission leads them to an encounter with a mysterious organisation that promises to hold back death itself - as well as a showdown with an alliance of old foes who have adopted unexpected new forms. Guest starring Michelle Gomez and Chris Addison

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Doctor's old allies at UNIT return to help him do battle with the Cybermen Missy has unleashed on the world, with further legions poised to join the mechanical ranks. But saving the Earth will require a terrible sacrifice to be made. Jenna Coleman co-stars, with guest appearances from Sanjeev Bhaskar, Jemma Redgrave, Ingrid Oliver, Michelle Gomez and Chris Addison

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny threatens to cancel the shop owner's Christmas if she can't manage to get her life in order. With Stevie having secured herself new employment, Miranda decides to follow suit and get a job in an office - but it proves easier said than done. Meanwhile, her romantic situation is just as messy as her professional life. Can she and Gary remain just good friends? Miranda Hart, Patricia Hodge and Tom Ellis star

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary decides to join the dating scene so he can bring someone along to Stevie's birthday party, prompting Miranda to do the same. Are the two of them destined to find romance in the arms of strangers - or with each other? Meanwhile, the shop owner can't hide her embarrassment when Penny decides to run for the local council. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Tom Ellis and Patricia Hodge

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Buster and Dermot are convinced Cathy's boyfriend Boris is a vampire - and there's only one way to deal with vampires. Agnes awaits the valuation of an 'antique' vase that's been in the family for generations, and Father Damien is called away to tend to some depressed chickens

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes and the gang start the New Year by attempting a tough new health and fitness regime, but while Maria and Cathy work hard to get the gang into shape, a mysterious visitor to Finglas raises some difficult questions from Agnes and Winnie's past

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Three childish young men try to navigate through the hardships of life. Life is good for the funboys until Callum gets his first-ever girlfriend and the change in dynamic threatens both their friendship - and Jordan's life. A burgeoning relationship means that Callum no longer wants to play video games all weekend with the fellas. This proves too much for the others to bear. Comedy, with Rian Lennon and Ryan Dylan

programme.title: Funboys
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Ballymacnoose
programme.desc: Following a gas leak at his flat, Callum moves in with Lorcan and his father Sammy, widely regarded as Ballymacnoose's biggest rotter. Driven by a desire to no longer be a pushover and in a desperate attempt to get over his break-up with Gemma, Callum goes under Sammy's wing and unleashes his own 'inner swine'. Meanwhile, Jordan's two fathers are finally tying the knot and he becomes wrapped up in all the excitement of the wedding. Comedy, with Ryan Dylan and Rian Lennon

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: With Callum now unwelcome at Boggins' farm and his flat still out of bounds because of a gas leak, he turns once more to his oldest, dearest friend, Jordan. Things seem to be going well - Jordan's two fathers show Callum some much-needed sympathy and introduce him to the foreign concept of 'talking about his feelings.' Unfortunately this new-found bond doesn't go down so well with the other member of the house - Jordan. Meanwhile, Gemma brings home a pet and unwittingly causes Lorcan to relive some very painful memories. Comedy, with Ryan Dylan and Rian Lennon

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Kirsty Craib believes she has ADHD and has spent the last eight years trying to get assessed, without success. Many young women report experiencing the same struggles getting support for neurodiverse conditions. She meets others whose delayed diagnosis has impacted their lives, and asks whether social media is making it more difficult to get help

programme.title: Gaia: A Death on Dancing Ledge
programme.desc: Zara McDermott investigates a mysterious death from 2017, in which a 19-year-old from Dorset ran away from home in a state of distress. Her body would later be found in the undergrowth by a massive search operation, having died of hypothermia. The first episode examines the early clues that were discovered and the suspicion that fell on a childhood friend and his grandmother, before they were released without charge

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Flo wants Donna to move out and Gaz accidentally gets the wrong idea. Janet feels neglected by Jonny and decides to spice up their relationship by surprising him in her sexy underwear - but things don't go according to plan. Meanwhile, Louise meets a new man. Comedy, starring Ralf Little

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Ellis Horne investigates the effects Instagram has on mental health, meeting young people who feel their mental health has been negatively impacted by constant exposure to the perfect lives of others. She explores the role that influencers play in creating enviable lifestyles that are unobtainable to most, and asks experts how to have a healthier relationship with the social media platform

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. Clara's life is devastated by a sudden tragedy, and she seeks the Doctor's help in putting things right. This personal mission leads them to an encounter with a mysterious organisation that promises to hold back death itself - as well as a showdown with an alliance of old foes who have adopted unexpected new forms. Guest starring Michelle Gomez and Chris Addison

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Doctor's old allies at UNIT return to help him do battle with the Cybermen Missy has unleashed on the world, with further legions poised to join the mechanical ranks. But saving the Earth will require a terrible sacrifice to be made. Jenna Coleman co-stars, with guest appearances from Sanjeev Bhaskar, Jemma Redgrave, Ingrid Oliver, Michelle Gomez and Chris Addison

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: George spars in the boxing gym but things take a turn, Ian struggles with recent events, and Denise is urged to start treatment

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat plans a birthday dinner for Jasmine, Oscar wrestles with the feelings he has for both Josh and Jasmine, and Jack tries to get through to Denise

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian seeks redemption, Nicola is determined to help George, and the Slaters gather for the birthday dinner

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos erupts at the Vic, Chelsea's world is turned upside down, and George and Gina try to clear the air

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor return to the abandoned manor hoping for answers but the arrival of two mysterious figures leads to a dangerous chase. The verdict is given in court sooner than anyone expected and at school, Pip loses control earning herself a suspension. A crucial clue cracks the case wide open

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.title: Wedding Crashers (2005)
programme.desc: Two party-mad bachelors make the most of the romantic atmosphere as they gatecrash weddings to pick up women. With a catalogue of tall stories to tell guests, the pair are regularly the hit of the events they attend - until one of them falls for a bridesmaid and they both learn a few things about love. Comedy, starring Owen Wilson, Vince Vaughn, Christopher Walken, Jane Seymour, Rachel McAdams and Isla Fisher

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: After four years travelling the world spending fraudulently accrued money, it seems inevitable that Elliot will be deported into the hands of British police, who have put together an extensive case of his crimes. Once again though, he has other ideas. In truth, the one thing Elliot desires as much as freedom is a settled life. But even when he thinks he's found it, with a new home and friends, there is no escape from his trail of lies and deception. Eventually, justice seems set to catch up with him

programme.title: Gaia: A Death on Dancing Ledge
programme.desc: Zara McDermott discovers how three years before her death, Gaia was approached online by an older man who turned out to be at the centre of a world of webcamming and online grooming in Dorset. Despite multiple warnings from those closest to her, Gaia wanted to help him and they became close

programme.title: Gaia: A Death on Dancing Ledge
programme.desc: Zara McDermott revisits the case of 19-year-old Gaia Pope-Sutherland, highlighting her family's search for truth and justice in the wake of her untimely death from hypothermia. Gaia's alleged attacker contacted her 10 months before she went missing. As her attempts to get a restraining order were declined, her state of mind deteriorated, requiring her to be sectioned under the Mental Health Act. The inquest into Gaia's death - one of the longest in British history - revealed vital clues into her final weeks, unearthing over 50 missed opportunities in her care and search for her

programme.title: The Idaho Murders: Trial by TikTok
programme.desc: Zara McDermott visits Moscow, Idaho to uncover the story of the social media frenzy that followed the murders of four Idaho University students. She meets with online true crime sleuths building their own theories about the murders and those who were personally affected by the social media storm as she reflects on her own role in the tragedy and learns more about obsessive online consumption of true crime

programme.title: Queen & Slim (2019)
programme.desc: A black couple's first date takes a shocking turn when a traffic stop leads to the killing of the police officer in self-defence, and the fugitive pair hit the road for a life-changing journey. When a video of the incident goes viral, the unwitting outlaws soon become a symbol of trauma, terror, grief and pain for people all across the United States. Crime drama, starring Daniel Kaluuya, Jodie Turner-Smith and Bokeem Woodbine

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Predator: The Billionaire Football Boss - Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: In their final challenge, the amateur cooks serve a three-course menu that delivers in presentation, taste and originality, hoping they will have done enough to impress Anna Haugh and Grace Dent and be crowned MasterChef Champion 2026. This will no doubt be a tight race as the finalists draw out every ounce of culinary know-how in the hope of coming out on top

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Pancake reluctantly agrees to a big birthday party, but finds his own way to handle it

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: A meteorite lands in the sea, and Dad claims it for science, but the animals want it back

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The ducklings ban Weasy from playing on their toy train track after he destroys it and it has to be rebuilt, so he creates his own mega course

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: When the heroes are faced with the Goblin King moving into the Big Birch, they decide to invent a quest that will soon have him running back home

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A rocket carries the cabin into space

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: While operating a lift, Duck and Frog create mayhem for the Mayor's grand opening

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Dot and Iono confront a trainer who has been playing pranks with his Stunfisk

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Kidzbop, Irish Dance Superstar and Women's World Cup Cricket!
programme.desc: Packed full of amazing celeb guests, epic challenges and lols galore!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Wonders of the Isle of Wight
programme.desc: Joe Crowley, Margherita Taylor and Adam Henson explore a side of the Isle of Wight few visitors ever see. Beyond the beaches and bustling holiday towns, the island's rare geology and southerly location have shaped a landscape rich in surprises, from unusual marine life and distinctive habitats to crops more often associated with warmer climates

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don brings a burst of colour to the jewel garden with vibrant annuals, plants out tender veg, and shares practical advice for tackling those greenhouse pests. Arit Anderson visits a striking garden in Greater Manchester packed with clever design solutions and wildlife-friendly planting that comes alive by day and night. In Dorset, Joe Swift explores a much-loved family garden reimagined for three generations. In Essex, a gardener has transformed a shaded, north-facing plot into a lush, lawn-free haven, while in Bristol, a Morris dancer channels movement and rhythm into her planting as she prepares for Gardeners' World Live in Birmingham

programme.title: The Great Caruso (1951)
programme.desc: Biopic of the acclaimed Italian operatic tenor Enrico Caruso, charting his progress from humble beginnings as a choirboy in Naples, to his difficult rise to fame in America, as he struggles to find acceptance before ultimately being recognised as one of the world's greatest singers. Starring Mario Lanza, Ann Blyth, Dorothy Kirsten and Jarmila Novotna

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of women during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team explores flats in Barwell in Leicestershire and London with short leases, and an end terrace in Larkfield, Kent, which have been snapped up at auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from an antiques fair in Builth Wells, and tries her hand at bog snorkelling in Llanwrtyd Wells. With experts Ben Cooper and Kate Bliss

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven (1960)
programme.desc: Oppressed Mexican villagers cross the border into America looking to buy guns to defend their homes and crops from bandits. Instead they encounter an enigmatic loner who persuades them to hire a band of gunslingers to teach the villagers how to fight before the marauders return. Western, with Yul Brynner, Steve McQueen, Charles Bronson, Robert Vaughn, James Coburn and Eli Wallach

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon assess items at Margam Country Park near Port Talbot, while presenter Paul Martin visits the birthplace of one of his heroes, a Welshman who took Hollywood by storm

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The truth about the Africanised honey bee, more commonly known as the killer bee, one of the most successful invaders and a cause of devastation in the natural world. The programme analyses whether there is a way of taming this lethal force of nature

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of this morning's military display from Horse Guards Parade in London, where the Colour of the King's Company, 1st Battalion Grenadier Guards, marked the King's official birthday. The spectacle of music, majesty and military precision culminated with the annual RAF flypast and the royal family's balcony appearance. With reports from Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: 40 Originals at the BBC: Volume 2
programme.desc: Another compilation of songs that became bigger hits for the artists who covered them, including Mick Jackson - who was the first to perform Blame it on the Boogie before his near namesake Michael and his brothers got hold of the song - and Liza Minnelli, who got to New York, New York before Frank Sinatra. Other artists featured include the Osmonds, Red Hot Chili Peppers, Randy Newman, Randy Crawford, David Bowie and Chaka Khan

programme.title: 40 Originals at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of 40 big hits by the artists that originally performed them, such as John Fogerty's Rockin' All Over the World - which became synonymous with 1970s rockers Status Quo - and Lori Lieberman's Killing Me Softly with His Song - covered by many artists, including Roberta Flack. Others featured include Gladys Knight, Neil Diamond, Nine Inch Nails, Dolly Parton and the Zutons, whose song Valerie became a massive hit for Amy Whitehouse

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford
programme.desc: As the weather gets warmer, a new tea plantation is under way on the banks of the lough in Portaferry. John's tour boat goes into the water for the season

programme.title: BlacKkKlansman (2018)
programme.desc: During the 1970s, Ron Stallworth, the first African-American police officer to serve in the Colorado Springs Police Department, attempts to infiltrate the local Ku Klux Klan branch with the assistance of Jewish cop Flip Zimmerman. Spike Lee's Bafta and Oscar-winning drama based on a true story, starring John David Washington and Adam Driver

programme.title: Nostalgia (2022)
programme.desc: A man returns to his hometown of Naples, 40 years after abruptly leaving as a teen, stirring up a past he has kept buried. He is determined to see his boyhood friend, now a leading Naples crime boss, with whom he shares a dark secret. Drama, starring Pierfrancesco Favino. In Italian

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: John and Janie arrive at a new age commune to investigate the murder of its founder. Janie hopes to solve the case quickly, as her estranged father is one of the members. Complicated feelings around him are brought to the fore, especially as John disregards her warnings not to trust her father

programme.title: Football's New Generation
programme.desc: The intimate story of England's newest recruits, including Cole Palmer, Kobbie Mainoo, Ollie Watkins and Marc Guéhi, charting their rise from grassroots heroes to Europe's biggest stage. The documentary hears from their loved ones and the veterans who mentored them, charting their paths from humble beginnings and revealing the personal battles that culminated in a new breed of England players at Euro 2024

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: George Anderson and Ruth Vichos are back in the Beechgrove Garden just outside Aberdeen, where they assess how the recent mixed climate has affected the crops and blooms. George is in the fruit cage checking for pests and pruning the Beechgrove fig, while Ruth is in her vertical plot, ideal for anyone with a small garden or little space to grow, where she will be planting a perennial that will give a crop year after year

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Rogart in Sutherland to find out about the challenges of modern crofting, while Shahbaz Majeed takes a walk to the Bone Caves of Inchnadamph in Assynt to see how an increase in visitor numbers is damaging the site. Leanna Fernandez visits a pond in the grounds of Castle Fraser in Aberdeenshire that has become a haven for rare damselfies and dragonflies, Rosie discovers what is being done to protect some of the UK's last surviving elm trees and Cammy Wilson meets a young dairy farmer who got her big break into the industry through a unique opportunity

programme.title: MOTD: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Haiti v Scotland. Another chance to see both teams' opening Group C match, held at Boston Stadium in the USA

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt serves up a feast of food from Theo Randall, Ching-He Huang and Georgina Hayden. Angela Scanlon faces her food heaven or hell and there are clips of Rick Stein and the Hairy Bikers

programme.title: Strangers on a Train (1951)
programme.desc: A charming psychopath meets a tennis professional on a train and suggests that they each commit a murder on the other's behalf. However, when the deranged man goes through with his part of the deal, the sportsman faces a race against time to prove his innocence. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller, based on the novel by Patricia Highsmith, starring Robert Walker, Farley Granger and Ruth Roman

programme.title: Dial M for Murder (1954)
programme.desc: A former tennis champion suspects his wife is having an affair, so he hatches a plan to have her killed and inherit her fortune. He succeeds in blackmailing a disgraced former soldier into murdering her and making it look like a burglary. However, the plan backfires when the intended victim kills the would-be assassin. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller, starring Ray Milland, Grace Kelly, Robert Cummings and John Williams

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by pioneering film director Alfred Hitchcock, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction. Paul Martin presents

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The autumn sales are over and preparations are being made for winter. In Newtonmore in Cairngorms National Park, Robert MacKenzie needs to gather in all his cows back down to the croft for pregnancy testing, while on North Uist, the MacDonalds are harvesting potatoes grown in the sandy machair soil unique to the west of Scotland

programme.title: Expedition Killer Whale
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at the hunting behaviours of killer whales in Antarctica, as a team of scientists and film-makers travel across freezing waters to find out more about these animals. Their mission is not only to deepen viewers' understanding of their hunting behaviours, but also to learn more about the more tender side of the killer whales

programme.title: The Game - The Story of Hurling
programme.desc: The documentary explores eras of great change - not just for hurling but for Ireland as a whole. Newspapers gave way to radio and in the 1960s TV broadcasting began. Hurling had a new medium and new audiences, but the two pillars of an Irish Sunday - church and the match - were no longer the only distractions and Ireland of the late 1960s was beginning to look very different

programme.title: Chronicles of the Sperrins
programme.desc: Peatland officer Rory is delighted to receive help with restoration work on the Black Bog and Bryan has secured permission to observe an ancient protected species of pearl mussels

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve joins shark scientist Mauricio Hoyos at a remote volcanic island in the eastern Pacific Ocean to discover where shark mothers give birth and help protect a new generation of the ocean giants

programme.title: The Mother of All Cons
programme.desc: On the day of Megan's funeral, the service is suddenly cancelled as the authorities want to carry out another post-mortem and rumours circulate that the police are now involved. The coroner's inquest concludes Megan died of natural causes and there is no mention of a brain tumour, but some of Megan's closest friends are baffled. If Megan wasn't ill, why did she show symptoms and why was she taking such enormous quantities of medication?

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Taking to the stage at Alexandra Palace Theatre in north London are Lola Young, Baby Rose, Wasia Project, Bonnie Prince Billy, Beverly Glenn-Copeland & Elizabeth Copeland, and Mike D, who also chats to Jools

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon has one chance to tackle a life-threatening tumour in his patient's liver and diaphragm. A neurosurgeon must save her patient from paralysis with unerring precision

programme.title: The Power of the Dog (2021)
programme.desc: In 1925, wealthy Montana ranch-owner Phil Burbank inspires fear and awe in those around him. When his brother George brings a new wife and her teenage son to live at their homestead, Phil torments them - until he finds himself exposed to the possibility of love. Jane Campion's Western, starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Kirsten Dunst and Jesse Plemons

programme.title: Southbank at 75: You Are Here
programme.desc: An epic dance spectacular to mark London's Southbank Centre's anniversary, forming an electrifying journey through 75 years of British youth culture and featuring the music of Shirley Bassey, the Verve, Bronski Beat and EsDeeKid among others. A freewheeling journey of discovery across decades, remixing and reimagining some of the most vivid and influential youth and social movements that have driven culture forward since 1951 when the south bank of the Thames hosted the Festival of Britain. Rebellion meets euphoria as a cast of hundreds take viewers on a trip through northern soul, dancehall and disco, and from Teddy Boys to the heady intensity of a nightclub dancefloor

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and a team of experts head into a remote part of the Moukalaba-Doudou National Park. Cut off from the outside world by the Doudou mountains, this zone has never been explored before. Teaming up with local jungle guide Ghislain Bouassa and primatologist Alessandra Mascaro, Steve hopes to discover whether this unexplored jungle could be home to an undiscovered population of chimpanzees

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts channels his inner Captain Mainwaring at a mid-terrace in Warminster in Wiltshire, Dion Dublin feels like he is literally floating in a mystery room in Cheshire, and the team are treated to their very own musical composition in Margate

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine presents as the reds and blues go shopping for collectibles in a jam-packed antiques centre in the Herefordshire town of Leominster under the guidance of experts Charles Hanson and Richard Madley

programme.title: Making Life on Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: The making of the acclaimed wildlife series that took three years to film, featuring 40 countries and over 600 species. First broadcast in 1979 and watched by 500 million people, it launched David Attenborough's career as arguably the most successful and influential wildlife film-maker of all time

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath embarks on a journey back to the mystical county of Cornwall to explore a monastery perched atop a holy island and the tomb of a legendary Cornish giant who once instilled fear in a local town

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and co-host Sarah Cruddas examine the Chicago O'Hare International Airport case of November 2006 - the sighting of a flying saucer hovering over a United Airlines airport gate. The incident was witnessed by multiple aviation professionals, pilots and passengers. The immediate aftermath of the event was captured in real time by audio recordings of communication between the airport's Air Traffic Control tower and airline staff, but the sighting was dismissed by the US government's Federal Aviation Administration as a weather phenomenon. Craig and Sarah talk to UFO investigator Robert Powell, who reveals the UFO punched a circular hole in the clouds as it departed upwards after hovering over the terminal. Craig and Sarah also learn astonishing details about how aviation authorities handled the incident

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.desc: Tony Harris separates the real from the fake in some of the most compelling Bigfoot evidence recently captured. Footprint casts from Mount St Helens may be proof that a massive, undiscovered primate lives in North America

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A man brings in a 160-year-old Tiffany watch, which has no identifying markings other than the name on its face. The pressing question is how the company verify that it is genuine

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The price of gold begins to fall, and the staff worry that they could end up losing money. Les, Seth and Ashley become more conscious of what items they buy

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les offers to buy a diamond-encrusted watch from a customer. He makes sure his jewellery expert takes a close look, having been stung by a similar deal previously

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Welwyn Garden City sort the treasures from their trash, offering them the chance to put items up for auction or accept a cash sum

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.sub-title: Big Cow Trouble in Little Ajax
programme.desc: The grand opening of Paul and Bogart's store finally arrives, but their plans suffer a setback when a new buyer shows up on the scene with money to burn

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Jeff Schwarz, aka The Liquidator, takes his place as guest auctioneer

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Patrick Pecker attends the auction, fuelling his long-standing feud with Roy

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: At storage auctions across Canada, high-stakes buyers convene daily. Here, Roy suits up in full army gear to declare war on the buyers in Scarborough

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Roy comes to the auction in Scarborough bearing 'gifts', but no one is impressed. Rick tries his best to turn Cindy's frown upside-down

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.sub-title: The Seven Habits of Highly Effective Investigations
programme.desc: The Thornhill buyers run for cover when a self-help CD gives Roy a new lease on his egomaniacal life

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Throughout history, humans have buried their dead in caves or chambers, in pyramids or in some form of a tomb. Ancient tombs were visual memorials for the dead. In China, a vast necropolis was constructed as a way to connect to the afterlife. The presenter questions the need for modern man to physically dig up the past and contemplates the secrets that might remain hidden in tombs

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: An exploration of ancient technologies that seem too advanced for their time, questioning if modern innovations merely reinvented long-lost creations and where those inventions disappeared

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: 'Death shall come on swift wings to him who disturbs the peace of the King' so the famous inscription goes. A mysterious curse haunts the original discoverers of Tutankhamun.

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Investigating a strange creature seen in a wooded area of rural Massachusetts by three different people who all gave similar descriptions. Could the so-called Dover Demon be a never before discovered animal or could it have come from another civilisation?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: A hoard of golden coins are found in the prospecting district of California, and a similarly valuable find in an old piano in rural England

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The discovery of a mysterious 500-year-old manuscript, with almost 300 pages full of handwritten notes and illustrations

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Alien Pilots, Carolina Lights and Stone-Cold Animals
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things 'caught on film'. This episode features footage purportedly of alien pilots in a UFO cockpit. Plus, the claims that a Australian man stumbled upon an alien corpse on the pavement

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Mystery Sea Beast, The Earth Is Alive and Shapeshifting UFO
programme.desc: A mysterious sea beast seen from the shore of a small seaside town in the UK. Plus, the notion that a field of massive mysterious jars were made by giants. Tony Harris and his team of experts examine the clues and make a determination about the validity of what they are being told

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Hairy Serpents, South Pole Nazis and the Horrible Blob
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts probe reports of a serpent-like creature caught on camera. Plus, whether the discovery of an ancient antenna found beneath the waters of Antarctica offers proof that the Nazis once colonised the South Pole

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A dagger sealed in King Tut's tomb for over 3,000 years is made of a possibly extra-terrestrial metal. Plus, a huge swastika found in the desert near Roswell

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A strange mass breaches the ocean surface near Antarctica - is it a sea monster? Ancient rock carvings in a remote mountain range depict unearthly beings

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A heavily armoured force invades one of Syria's most ancient archaeology sites, prompting fears its relics are being stolen

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: The seafloor of the Atlantic is littered with mysteries, prompting professional salvage diver Tony Sampson and his team to brave the shifting currents and bone-chilling legends of Nova Scotia in search of answers. They begin with the SS Atlantic. Back in April 1873, while its captain slept, the Atlantic smashed at full speed into the rocky shores, resulting in the deaths of around 550 people

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: Professional salvage diver Tony Sampson and his team brave the shifting currents and bone-chilling legends of Nova Scotia in search of wreckage. Here, they turn their attentions to the Ghost Ship of Northumberland Strait, a ghost ship said to sail ablaze within the body of water that separates Prince Edward Island from Nova Scotia and New Brunswick in eastern Canada

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler
programme.desc: The actor highlights some culinary curiosities, as he revisits some of the most surprising and sometimes alarming food trends from the past. Examples include radioactive energy drinks that once seemed like the next big thing, scandalous snacks made with Olestra and dubious additives in milk and bread. Henry sheds light on how certain foods came to be and the consequences they had

programme.title: Hitler's Handmaidens
programme.desc: Eva Braun is perhaps Adolf Hitler's most famous girlfriend - but she was not his first lover. Piecing together a baffling parade of young women - some related to him - Hitler is revealed as a sexually perverse and often absent lover. Mysteriously, many girlfriends died in mysterious circumstances, and conspiracy theories abound

programme.title: Hitler's Secret Sex Life
programme.desc: The myriad of rumours, theories and disputed historical accounts of Adolf Hitler's sexual psychology, considering how it integrated into his overall twisted psyche. This series addresses various time periods of Hitler's alleged and proven sex life and explores the role sex played in shaping Hitler's behaviour

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath explores Roman Wales, visiting ancient sites and tracing Welsh Druidry's final stand on the Isle of Anglesey. He discovers a hidden museum and hears a tale from folklorist Maxine Allinson about St. David's miraculous birth. In Carmarthen, he connects with Merlin's Oak and learns about local physicians. His journey ends in Snowdonia, where he investigates the battle behind the mountain's name

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained
programme.desc: Thousands of UFO sightings are reported every year, along with terrifying abductions and other bizarre occurrences. This series examines these accounts and explores recently declassified top secret UFO files, casting new light on historic cases

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Reports of vanishing aircraft and UFOs have convinced investigators that the US government knows more than it admits in the remote deserts of Utah. The top-secret military base Area 52 may hold the answers

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: For decades UFO research has been directed to the skies. However, newly declassified documents, along with advances in technology are leading researchers to believe that UFOs may also be inhabiting the planet's oceans

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence

programme.title: Medieval Murder Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Amy Dudley: The Queen, Her Lover, and His Wife
programme.desc: The suspicious death of Elizabethan noblewoman Amy Dudley, the wife of Robert Dudley, the favourite and alleged lover of Queen Elizabeth I and the man the monarch is said to have wanted to marry - if only he had been available. Amy's body was found at the foot of the stairs at Cumnor Place, her home in Oxfordshire in 1560, and her death caused a scandal - with the finger of blame pointed firmly at Dudley

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes on a four-day trek through the Kintail region of the north-western Highlands, examining the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s along the way. He starts by visiting Iron Age dwellings in Shiel Bridge, and heads to the rebuilt castle on Eilean Donan, the original of which was destroyed after invading Spanish troops landed there and were attacked by British warships. Tony ends in Glen Shiel, the site of a climactic battle in 1719 where royalist troops defeated the rebels

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire, from Avebury to Stonehenge, telling the story of the remarkable development that occurred in the latter days of the Neolithic era. His route over chalk downlands and Salisbury Plain takes him through one of the greatest concentrations of prehistoric sites in Europe

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy two mannequins, a Moorcroft vase, an electric massager, a piece of religious art and a curious ladder. With Nigel Havers

programme.title: Jojo Rabbit (2019)
programme.desc: A lonely German boy discovers that his single mother is hiding a Jewish girl in their attic. Aided only by his imaginary friend, Adolf Hitler, Jojo Rabbit must confront his blind nationalism as the Second World War continues to rage on. Taika Waititi wrote, directed and co-stars in this Oscar-winning comedy, featuring Roman Griffin Davis, Thomasin McKenzie, Rebel Wilson, Stephen Merchant and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: It's the grand final, and at the end of this episode, one of the five competitors will walk away with a trophy that looks very little like Greg Davies tucked under their arm. But before that, Amy Gledhill literally tears her hair out, Armando Iannucci plays the recorder while riding a trike with a plunger on his head, Joel Dommett sniffs the contents of a vacuum, Kumail Nanjiani mistreats a beloved resident of the Taskmaster house and Joanna Page lays waste to Little Alex Horne. Plus, the greatest pillow fights of all time

programme.title: The Hate U Give (2018)
programme.desc: Starr Carter lives in two worlds - the poor, black neighbourhood where she resides and the mostly white prep school she attends. When she witnesses the fatal shooting of her childhood friend by a policeman, Starr must find her voice and stand up for what's right. Drama, starring Amandla Stenberg, Regina Hall and Russell Hornsby

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Wharfe House in Wetherby, run by B&B newcomer Pauline Cox, who's joined by her best friend Claire Smith. Eyebrows are raised when Mike and Karen Hepker arrive from Bath Lodge Castle in a chauffeur-driven car. During a visit to a local vineyard, sparks fly between Mike Hepker and Tash Thorne, who runs The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport with her partner Mike Pearce. Having only just opened, Pauline has a house full for the very first time. The narrow antique beds cause concerns and Jenny and Clive Aylett from Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk are first to criticise breakfast. When it comes to feedback, Pauline's left deflated by her sausage scores - and Karen and Mike Hepker vote with their feet

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners visit Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk, run by husband-and-wife duo Clive and Jenny Aylett, but when the pair's rivals arrive, Pauline Cox and Claire Smith have difficulty locating their favourite appliance. Publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne are astonished to discover they cannot lock their room's door. In the morning, some of the guest make awkward requests to change their breakfast orders, causing professional chef Clive to experience a meltdown in the kitchen that leads to a fundamental error

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Bath Lodge Castle in Somerset for the third day of the competition, where they are wowed by Karen and Mike Hepker's luxury castle experience. Although the guests are disappointed at the bathroom fixtures and fittings, they are given the chance to vent their emotions during an afternoon of painting in the castle grounds. Plus, tensions rise between Mike and Tash at dinner, and harsh comments at feedback lead the hosts to suspect game playing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport, Hampshire, run by publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne. Karen Hepker is disappointed with the honeymoon suite, while Clive and Jenny Aylett find a dubious looking splash guard in the shower. A boat tour backfires when Jenny struggles with her water phobia and at dinner Karen takes the opportunity to tell the others exactly what she thinks of them. But things really kick off when it comes to feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wharfe House hosts Pauline Cox and Claire Smith begin a bitter debate, demanding to know why their beds caused such a sleepless night. Accusations subsequently fly in all directions and walk-outs look on the cards as tensions mount leading to the payments being revealed

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: The Heffernans begin attending the gym with The Incredible Hulk star Lou Ferrigno and his wife, but Doug is less than impressed with the arrangement and makes a secret pact with the actor that enables him to relax instead of working out. Kevin James stars

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie reminisces about why she chose to marry Doug, but also learns some uncomfortable truths, while Danny falls for Holly after his girlfriend leaves him

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug bumps into his erstwhile football coach while delivering a parcel to his old high school, and Arthur loses his senses of sight, hearing and speech when his eye drops go missing. Comedy, guest starring Burt Reynolds with Kevin James and Jerry Stiller

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie is depressed about celebrating her 35th birthday, so Doug asks builders to wolf whistle at her to boost her ego. Meanwhile, Arthur searches for the ideal gift for his daughter. American comedy, starring Kevin James, Leah Remini and Jerry Stiller

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray gives the eulogy at a family funeral, but his Aunt Alda spoils the solemnity of the event by rekindling a seven-year-old feud with his mother. American comedy, starring Ray Romano and Jean Stapleton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert asks Ray and Debra to help with his criminal psychology course by taking IQ tests - but gives them misleading results to gauge their reactions

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra becomes jealous of a waitress who catches Ray's eye. Although the situation is perfectly innocent, the couple soon blow everything out of all proportion

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: An unlikely sequence of events culminates in Martin pretending to be gay on Valentine's Day. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A temporary call-screener steals the show with her own brand of no-nonsense advice, leaving Frasier feeling insecure. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles' girlfriend Mel persuades him to compete against Frasier for the honour of becoming cork master at a local wine club, causing friction in the Crane household. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin helps trap an unfaithful husband, while Roz and Frasier attend a conference on behalf of the radio station, and find themselves with no choice but to share a room. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart discovers a previously unknown comet while carrying out a punishment for Principal Skinner. Unfortunately, to the horror of all concerned, it is on a direct collision course with Springfield - and Flanders owns the only bomb shelter in town

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer enrols in clown college and begins impersonating Krusty, but trouble looms on the horizon as the showbiz prankster's underworld creditors come looking for the money he owes them. Featuring the guest voices of Joe Mantegna, Dick Cavett and Johnny Unitas

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer joins the Stonecutters - a secret society responsible for just about everything that goes wrong in the world - and is hailed as their leader thanks to an ancient prophecy and a unequally shaped birthmark. Featuring the guest voice of Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Harboro Hotel in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, During a pie-making class, two B&Bers intrigue everyone with their hospitality inexperience

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Nest Hotel in Hampshire, father and daughter Shane and Emily hope to dazzle with the decor. An afternoon spent at an ancient farm descends into mudslinging and the hosts end up feeling that they've been done dirty at feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Visit three this week is to The Queen's Head Inn in Northamptonshire, where B&B novices Simon and Harriet think their old-school village pub will be a hit but the bacon gets a bashing at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Worlington Hall in Suffolk, married couple Louis and Michael lay on luxurious interiors and take their guests for a 'mill around a mill'. Breakfast is an opulent affair and the B&Bers are left hailing their hosts. However, one pair are perturbed by Louis and Michael's lavish approach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The holiday hosts meet one final time on payment day to clash over cleanliness and spar over sleep scores, before the payments are revealed and we find out who has won

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sandy and Andy dream of eventually retiring to the lakes and mountains of Lazio in Italy, but for now want to use a property there to share holidays with their sizable family of five children and six grandchildren. Danni Menzies works with a budget of £70,000 to hunt out a traditional-style house with plenty of space and a big enough garden to grow fruit and vegetables

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in Edinburgh helping house-hunters who want to be in the capital's most popular enclaves. Kirstie is with Northern Irishman Rob and his Mexican wife Maria, who want out of their noisy rented tenement flat, and into a house of their own where their one-year-old daughter Olivia can flourish. Phil is with James and Lesley, who is living with ME, or Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, which has affected their search. Lesley's condition impacts what they need in a property and also how often they've been able to go out to viewings, so Phil shows them various options that suit their future-proofing requirements

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp catches up with Kathleen and Tom to see how far they have come with the renovations to their four-bedroom Newcastle home. The couple first appeared on the show back in 2016 when their three-bedroom Victorian terrace in Gosforth, had become a four-year Love It or List It battleground. In 2019, Phil Spencer returned and learnt that the couple had made the leap to the spacious home they now reside in

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.desc: Lee McKenzie presents action from the battle for grid positions for the seventh round of the season at Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya in Spain. The Spanish Grand Prix was held here last year and the McLaren's Oscar Piastri took pole position, with his teammate Lando Norris joining him at the front of the grid. With analysis from David Coulthard and Billy Monger

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: In Hornsea, new recruit Liam joins an independent lifeboat. Can he pass the gruelling tests that will enable him to save lives at sea? In Burton Constable, we discover the remains of an ocean giant washed up on the Holderness shoreline that appears in the literary classic Moby Dick. Glampsite owners Ed and Verity create a bespoke tent with glorious views of Whitby's gothic abbey

programme.title: Where the Crawdads Sing (2022)
programme.desc: Catherine 'Kya' Clark is a young woman who fends for herself in the Deep South. Chase Andrews courts her in opposition to his mother, but when he is found dead, the finger of suspicion points at Kya, so lawyer Tom Milton mounts a robust defence. Drama adapted from Delia Owens' bestseller, starring Daisy Edgar-Jones, David Strathairn and Taylor John Smith

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including The Inheritance, You Bet! on Tour, KPopped, Emmerdale, The Guest and 90 Day Fiancé UK. They also review a This Morning feature on roast dinners and ITV News coverage of the resignation of Angela Rayner as deputy prime minister

programme.title: Iris Prize Best British Shorts
programme.desc: Jack Howard's short film nominated for the Iris Prize film award in 2025. A young waiter is confronted by a mysterious older woman on a rainy night

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Baltimore, Maryland, where Denise Whiting has alienated customers by trademarking the word 'hon', a term of endearment used by the local community. When she opened Café Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales

programme.title: Make That Movie
programme.desc: Loyal chauffeur Kabir wants the team to tell the life story of his boss, hard-working financier Mr Eblen Archer. Sam thinks it seems like such a heart-warming idea, until he realises there isn't much of a story to tell. However, Pat knows the rich and famous always have sordid secrets, so the team go in search of scandals their subject is involved in

programme.title: Make That Movie
programme.desc: A couple become trapped in an underground cave, so Sam and his crew respond immediately to the national emergency by planning a movie that they can send down to lift their spirits. However, to complicate matters, an actual rescue team are also on site, who keep getting in the movie-makers' way - and who might just save the couple before the movie is finished. Offbeat comedy, written by and starring Sam Campbell

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Comedian and podcaster Adam Buxton joins Joe Lycett to assess the wonders of Prague, capital of the Czech Republic. A kayak tour along the River Vltava allows them to take in the key sights, including the castle and the Charles Bridge. They then explore the work of sculptor David Cerný, whose controversial pieces adorn the city. Dinner is served at Levitate, where they eat their way through an 18-course tasting menu, including eating a sausage out of a skull and sampling caviar-laced ice-cream. On day two, the pair enjoy a beer spa before ascending the Zizkov TV Tower, for a bird's eye view of the city

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Ashford, Kent, francophiles Stephanie and Ian kick of the competition by serving French food from a home emulating the Palace of Versailles. The second night sees city slickers Louise and Dan attempt to secure their lead with spreadsheet organisation, before fun-loving couple Pippa and Lee make a bid for the prize with a mixture of fancy dress, stripping off, wacky voices and homely food

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart discovers a previously unknown comet while carrying out a punishment for Principal Skinner. Unfortunately, to the horror of all concerned, it is on a direct collision course with Springfield - and Flanders owns the only bomb shelter in town
