programme.title: Jamie's Great Britain
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver tours the length and breadth of the nation to explore the origins of its most popular dishes, and discovers how favourites from abroad were created. In the first edition, he travels to the East End of London to uncover his family's roots and to sample modern Vietnamese street food

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James and fellow chef Mark Jordan discover the culinary delights of Guernsey. They sample local speciality Gache cake and a seafood platter fit for a king, while James uses the island's native dairy produce to make a creamy rice pudding with chargrilled peaches. The duo set themselves a fishing challenge by attempting to catch a mighty turbot, and James finishes the day by cooking a mouthwatering turbot dish

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: The chef is joined by Paul Ainsworth to explore the culinary delights of Dorset, with the pair going aboard a millionaire's yacht, where James cooks a sea bass dish. Paul samples local cheeses, while James visits a breeder with Mangalitsa pigs in a beautiful coastline setting

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: The celebrated seafood chef and his sidekick Chalky take another culinary romp around Britain, sampling such delights as handmade pickles, lamb pies, smoked kippers and damson cobblers and testing whether free range eggs taste better than battery produce

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: The celebrated seafood chef and sidekick Chalky reveal what life is like on the road before they attend a food festival in the Yorkshire Dales, encounter a charismatic fish merchant in Edinburgh and discover the secrets of a good Bloody Mary. A visit to a walled garden also inspires Rick to cook soupe au pistou

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: Rick Stein enjoys the delights of freshwater crayfish, hop asparagus and cider brandy as he continues his gastronomic tour. Along the way, chef Shaun Hill reveals his passion for Ludlow's local cuisine, and Chalky finally gets his paws on a rabbit

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: The celebrated seafood chef and sidekick Chalky visit Cumbria, where they sample a sticky toffee pudding that saved a local shop, as well as Welsh sea trout and tasty sausages from free-range pigs. They also sample a surprising pork brawn that leaves Rick lost for words

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: Rick explores the gastronomic delights of Ireland, paying a return visit to Cork's English Market. He goes on to taste soda bread straight from the oven, before cooking classic Irish recipes such as beef and oyster pie with Guinness, and corned beef with cabbage

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: Rick meets the Nute family of Tintagel, who grow potatoes the old-fashioned way, and samples a few tipples at the Newton Abbot Beer Festival. Then it's on to Yorkshire, where he samples award-winning herbs cultivated by Jekka McVicar, before heading for Cornwall's Cadgwith Cove

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: The celebrated seafood chef visits Smithfield Market, where he challenges the resident butchers to taste the difference between organic beef and off-the-shelf supermarket products

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: The seafood chef witnesses the best and worst of the fishing industry as he continues his culinary romp around Britain, tasting delicious Scottish prawns that are protected by the Marine Stewardship Council, before learning of the dumping of prime monkfish off the Cornish coast

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes
programme.desc: The seafood chef visits Jersey with his faithful dog Chalky, where they spend time reef fishing for sea bass, and enjoy a delicious Madeiran feast in St Helier, before travelling back to Cornwall, arriving just in time for a pheasant shoot

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig present the semi-final of the baking contest, which tests the four remaining bakers' skills with patisserie. There are only three places in next week's final, and this round of challenges will determine who does not make the cut. The signature dish requires a delicate dome and a perfect finish, while for the technical, the bakers must take croquembouche to the next level. Finally, for the showstopper, they must use all their skills to construct an edible display case

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host the final of the culinary competition. As family and friends gather on the lawn outside, the last three bakers enter the tent to prepare a chocolate cake as their signature bake. For the technical challenge, they must master that most delicate of dishes, the souffle, and finally for their showstopper, they must create an edible illusion using only cake, sweet bread and biscuits

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas takes over from Sandi Toksvig as host for the 2020 series, alongside returning presenter Noel Fielding and regular judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith. The 12 contestants for this series have been required to form a social bubble due to Covid-19 lockdown restrictions. The contest begins with cake week, beginning with a signature challenge to produce a classic Battenberg. For the technical round, Paul sets the contestants the task of making a fruit sponge, and the final showstopper challenge gives the bakers a chance to express their creativity

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding present biscuit week. The signature challenge involves a sophisticated combination of fruit, nut and chocolate, and the bakers must then produce two takes on a tropical technical. For the showstopper round, they are challenged to create an elaborate table setting made entirely from biscuits, before judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith decide who will be crowned Star Baker and who will be leaving the contest this week

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: In Bilbao, James visits a huge indoor food market, takes a funicular to a famous steak restaurant, and sees how La Carolina is made. Later, he heads to a winery on a seabed, before cooking up dishes including squid ink arancini and Basque cheesecake

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels to North Carolina, where his challenge is to eat 17 overstuffed hot dogs in less than 30 minutes

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman continues his oversized culinary journey with a German-themed challenge in Minneapolis. His task is to consume a metre of bratwurst and sauerkraut

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Still in Hampshire, James views ex-formula one racer Jody Scheckter's epic car collection, and learns of his passion for buffalo mozzarella, which he makes in his dairy. James uses some of the cheese in his buffalo mozzarella, blackberry and beetroot salad, before crossing into Sussex to don fancy dress and experience the Goodwood Revival, which celebrates motorsport from the 1940s to the 1960s. The presenter offers a classic comfort recipe - roast pork with black pudding fritters and caramelised apples as he prepares to watch some of his friends race in the Goodwood Revival TT race

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits a Southwestern restaurant in Colorado, a Po' Boy house in Louisiana, and tries a pork and kale sandwich in Indiana

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri samples food in unique American eateries, including caramel pecan roll in Idaho, English breakfast in California and burgers in Connecticut

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri samples a unique take on pork tacos in San Antonio, before he heads to New Orleans to try pork knuckles

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is exploring an entire world of barbecue. After sampling chicken salad in Delaware, he heads to Kauai for a traditional Hawaiian pig roast

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Delaware, Guy Fieri digs into arancini and veal scaloppini, and samples falafels in San Diego

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri gets a taste of international flavours. A true British pub serves authentic English staples, while a Mexican spot is spicing up shrimps

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates a range of recipes using produce from Britain's farms and gardens, from a pasta inspired by a classic French herb sauce, to a perfect way to partner asparagus, and her own version of a classic tarte Tatin. She also travels on one of the Watercress Line's 19th-century steam trains, where she leaves the stove to help fire up the 300-ton engine

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates a range of recipes for entertaining guests, including beetroot gravadlax, sticky chicken, crispy roast duck, and raspberry mousse. Mary also travels to Leeds Castle in Kent with acclaimed florist Simon Lycett as he prepares as he prepares for the Festival of Flowers

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes some mind-blowing treats for her latest blog post. Tasty s'mores cupcakes, stunning marbled-dipped apples and deep-fried cheesecake bites

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes Ladd and Chuck a hearty lunch as they dismantle a historic cattle feed storehouse. She makes steak and eggs with Hollandaise sauce on garlic English muffins

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Glazed Pork Fillet and Tapas Bruschetta
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver prepares a glazed pork fillet accompanied by Cajun-style pepper rice and barbecue sauce, plus a tapas bruschetta and golden grilled sardines

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Lamb Kofte and Keralan Veggie Curry
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver continues to demonstrate his own brand of fast food, showing how to prepare lamb kofte, pitta and Greek salad, and shares a recipe for Keralan vegetarian curry, poppadoms, rice and minty yoghurt

programme.title: Be My Guest with Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina Garten's friends Rob Marshall and John DeLuca sip watermelon cosmopolitans. Plus, John makes salmon burgers with his mum's simplest slaw

programme.title: No Way Out (1987)
programme.desc: A navy officer unwittingly has an affair with a politician's mistress, and becomes involved in the investigation after she is murdered. However, he soon suspects the plan is to find a scapegoat rather than the real killer - and that he is the most likely candidate to take the fall. Political thriller, starring Kevin Costner, Gene Hackman, Sean Young, Iman and George Dzundza

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: For years John has thrilled his family with tales of his old army friend Tip. However, when he pays a visit to Walton's Mountain, doubts creep in as to whether the children's hero is all he's cracked up to be after he starts a fire and accidentally shoots a dog

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Jim Bob ends up in hospital with appendicitis, forcing Grandpa and John-Boy to rely on a famous author to help them complete an important contract

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Cousins Ham, Cora and Job come to stay on Walton's Mountain, but they prove to be out-and-out crooks

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: John-Boy receives a visit from cousin Homer, but the Baldwin sisters are unaware of his criminal record until he makes off with much of the stock for their reunion banquet recipe

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: After meeting an itinerant singer, a restless Mary Ellen decides to run away from home. Meanwhile, the rest of the family works hard picking apples to save enough money to send her on a trip to Washington to cure her wanderlust

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: A famous film star is stranded on the mountain when her car breaks down and is invited to stay with the Waltons, putting on a one-woman show to raise her train fare back to the city. While she charms most of the family, Olivia is not convinced by her tall tales. Pippa Scott and Dorothy Neuman guest star

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: A girl befriended by the family is shocked to learn her drunken father was responsible for burning down the school to prevent Miss Hunter teaching the theory of evolution

programme.title: The Waltons

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Olivia's timid uncle Cody Nelson pays a visit to the family after losing his job in a bank, and John-Boy and Grandpa set out to find him a wife. However, he falls in love with Cordelia Hunnicutt, who has been married four times already, much to the disapproval of Olivia and Grandma

programme.title: The Horse Whisperer (1998)
programme.desc: A young girl is left disabled and her horse profoundly traumatised after a horrific road accident. Her distraught mother decides to take them to the remote Montana ranch of an equine healer. As time passes, his therapy and the peace and quiet of the countryside work their magic on their broken spirits. Drama based on Nicholas Evans' novel, directed by and starring Robert Redford. With Kristin Scott Thomas, Sam Neill, Dianne Wiest and Scarlett Johansson

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.sub-title: One of Our Running Backs Is Missing
programme.desc: Steve finds himself caught up in a battle of the giants when a professional footballer is kidnapped. Seventies drama, starring Lee Majors

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve is furious when his protests are ignored and Barney Miller, the seven million dollar man who experienced a nervous breakdown, is reactivated. It is soon clear the bionic basket-case is not firing on all cylinders when he injures a race-car owner and embarks on a life of crime. Can Steve track him down and put a stop to his misdeeds?

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve takes the place of a missing agent on the trail of smugglers trafficking in supersensitive detection devices. Action drama, starring Lee Majors

programme.desc: A comet collides with Earth, causing a devastating series of quakes, floods and volcanic eruptions. However, when the dust has settled, it turns out worse is to come. The impact has pushed the planet off its axis, and if a team of scientists cannot put it right, the crust will shift, wiping out all life. Disaster thriller, starring Jack Coleman, Holly Dignard and Tyler Johnston

programme.desc: An army officer mounts a daring raid through the treacherous Florida swamplands. His mission is to take on a group of marauding Seminole rebels, as well as the gun runners who have been supplying them with weapons, and rescue a group of settlers who have been taken hostage by the tribe. Western, starring Gary Cooper, Mari Aldon, Richard Webb, Ray Teal and Arthur Hunnicutt

programme.title: Arrowhead (1953)
programme.desc: A cavalry scout witnesses a series of raids by an Apache tribe that is supposedly in peace talks - but his superiors reject his story out of hand and dismiss him from duty. Continuing to spy on the tribe, he comes to suspect the chief's son is behind the attacks. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Jack Palance and Katy Jurado

programme.title: The Appaloosa (1966)
programme.desc: A Mexican-American cowboy swears revenge on the bandit chieftain whose female accomplice and lover stole his prize Appaloosa stallion and ended his dream of owning a stud farm. A foray into the bandit camp leads to his humiliation and torture, until a final arm-wrestling duel with the leader offers a chance of survival. Western, with Marlon Brando, John Saxon, Anjanette Comer and Emilio Fernandez

programme.desc: A gambler plans to win a fortune in Las Vegas by utilising his boss's special powers

However, its unity is threatened when two of the men fall in love with the same woman. Western, starring James Van Der Beek, Dylan McDermott, Usher Raymond, Ashton Kutcher and Rachael Leigh Cook

programme.title: The Mask of Zorro (1998)
programme.desc: The sword-wielding saviour of Spanish California returns after 20 years in prison, tutoring a thief to take over his heroic role and tackle corrupt government officials, who are using slave labour to mine gold illegally. Both mentor and pupil have personal scores to settle with their enemies, led by a ruthless governor and a psychotic military officer. Swashbuckling adventure, with Anthony Hopkins, Antonio Banderas and Catherine Zeta-Jones.

programme.title: Basic Instinct (1992)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking San Francisco cop investigates the case of a man murdered with an ice-pick, but his normally well-honed intuition starts letting him down as he falls for the sultry and manipulative chief suspect, the victim's former girlfriend. Thriller from director Paul Verhoeven, starring Sharon Stone, Michael Douglas, George Dzundza and Jeanne Tripplehorn

programme.desc: A getaway driver crashes his car in Mexico, leading to the authorities seizing his ill-gotten gains and condemning him to a violent, lawless prison dominated by gangs. A boy who has also been locked up there proves an unlikely ally in helping him survive, and he hatches a plan to escape and recover his money. Crime thriller, starring Mel Gibson and Kevin Hernandez

programme.title: The Internecine Project (1974)
programme.desc: An economics professor is about to start a prestigious job as a government adviser - but his murky past as an industrial spy threatens his chances. His only way of burying the past is to dispose of his former associates, so he hatches a plan to trick them into murdering one another. Thriller, starring James Coburn, Lee Grant, Harry Andrews and Ian Hendry

Sci-fi drama which began the series, starring Michael O'Hare, Jerry Doyle and Mira Furlan

programme.title: Babylon 5
programme.desc: The Narns launch an unprovoked attack on a peaceful Centauri colony, and raiders target transport ships near the station

programme.title: Babylon 5
programme.desc: A damaged alien craft is brought on board the ship, and the crew identifies its inhabitant as the Soul Hunter, an immortal predator that can sense death

programme.title: Babylon 5
programme.desc: Londo's career is placed in jeopardy when an exotic dancer seduces him and steals his secret files. Meanwhile, Garibaldi investigates the unauthorised use of a restricted channel of communication

programme.title: Babylon 5
programme.desc: A xenoarchaeologist who was Franklin's mentor smuggles strange artefacts into the station, transforming his assistant into a rampaging monster

programme.title: George and the Dragon (2004)
programme.desc: A knight returns from the Crusades looking forward to a quiet life, but finds himself embarking on a new mission when the king's daughter disappears. He sets out to find the missing princess - in return for some land of his own - but his quest leads to a confrontation with a fearsome dragon. Fantasy adventure, with James Purefoy, Piper Perabo, Simon Callow, Patrick Swayze and Michael Clarke Duncan

programme.title: Mammoth (2006)
programme.desc: A sleepy town is thrown into turmoil when a meteor strikes the local museum, bringing a woolly mammoth residing in one of the display cases to life. The ancient beast goes on a violent rampage, leaving the residents with the task of hunting it down before it escapes into the outside world. Sci-fi thriller, starring Vincent Ventresca, Summer Glau, Tom Skerritt and Cole Williams

programme.title: Moby Dick (1956)
programme.desc: Obsessive skipper Captain Ahab leads his ship and crew on an epic quest to find and kill the legendary white whale that bit off his leg - but his lust for vengeance endangers all their lives. John Huston's seafaring adventure, based on Herman Melville's novel, starring Gregory Peck, Richard Baseheart, Orson Welles and Leo Genn

Courtroom drama, based on a novel by John Grisham and starring Matthew McConaughey, Sandra Bullock, Samuel L Jackson, Donald Sutherland and Kevin Spacey

Crime drama, with a script by James Ellroy, starring Keanu Reeves, Forest Whitaker and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics Jan Vann and Chelsie Kennedy rush to a road traffic collision, but find the driver of the car has left the vehicle and doesn't want to be treated by the crew. The police suspect he may have been drinking, but the crew need to convince him to receive their help. In Coventry, paramedics Neil and Laura rush to a two-year-old boy who is having a severe allergic reaction

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 52-year-old cyclist is rushed in to St George's Hospital in Tooting after being hit by a car, while a 92-year-old former Baptist minister is admitted after a fall while out walking. Meanwhile, a painter and decorator has suffered a wrist injury that is proving challenging to diagnose

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to save a 27-year-old diabetic woman who's trapped in a three-car pile-up on the M6. In Coventry, paramedics Neil Vann and Laura Perkins are called to an 82-year-old man with severe breathing problems, who is on the point of death and has left a note asking not to be resuscitated. Meanwhile in Birmingham, a crew rushes to save a man with a history of heart attacks who is suffering chest pains at work

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager is rushed to hospital with a lacerated wrist after putting his hand through a pane of glass, an incident which leaves his father reflecting on his family history. Another patient discusses being diagnosed with a chronic condition that causes constant stomach pain, and a 76-year-old woman suffers severe shortness of breath and a tight chest

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits a farm in Cornwall to get to the bottom of the 'flower' element in a in cauliflower. Helen Lawal is in Spain to find out what makes lemon juice last so long, while Matt Tebbutt gets the beers in at a Worksop brewery, as he explores what the term 'bottle conditioned' actually means

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owner Helen Marriott offers her guest a chance to 'step off the planet' and treats them to essential breathing lessons, a chance to get to know one another in the hot tub, and soul-soothing foot massages at the Fountains Court Holistic Health Hotel in the North Yorkshire resort of Scarborough

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to the Whittington & Cat, a pub in Hull that also offers B&B. Proprietor Sue Perkins treats her guests to an afternoon of ice hockey, but they are intrigued by the possible contents of her peculiarly titled breakfast menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Khurram Saba hosts the third day of the competition at his Saba Hotel in west London and finds his guests are expecting metropolitan chic, and certainly not fish and chips for dinner. Candida Leaver is extremely displeased when she experiences a sleepless night, while Sue Perkins and Gill take the opportunity to go out and have some fun

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Candida Leaver and Peter Starling, owners of the Bower Inn in Bridgwater, Somerset, play host in the last round of the week. The group activity is willow-weaving, and at dinner time the guests reveal what they think of their no-nonsense hosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: With their family grown up, football fans Catherine and Martin wish to fulfil their long-held dream of buying a holiday home in Italy. They've chosen the stunning Lazio coast as their perfect location and want a two-bedroom property close to the sea. Scarlette Douglas shows them five options to consider within their budget of £275,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps semi-retired builder Martyn and prison manager Sharon find their dream holiday home on Portugal's Algarve. On their budget of £160,000, they're looking for a year-round holiday home that all their friends and family can enjoy. Danni shows them five options, but will they love any of them enough to make an offer?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Jean Johansson helps Trish and her sister Ann find a holiday home along the coastline in southern Cyprus. Trish's dream is to find her perfect two-bedroomed property where she can holiday a couple of times a year with family and friends, and most importantly, enjoy the island's warm winters. With a budget of £90,000, Jean lines up five attractive options for the pair

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Recently retired Clover and Mark have been living in Manchester for 30 years, but have decided that it's time to find a new home and start a new life in the stunning white villages of Axarquía in Andalucía. With a budget of £300,000, they want their new forever home to be a house that works for them and allows their adult children to visit frequently. Danni Menzies lines up five private villas with pools and stunning views, for the pair to consider

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: in Cardiff, DJ Trishna hopes her cooking will be even better than her rapping, as she hosts a hip-hop themed night in a bid to scoop the £1000 prize. However, when Yuno shakes his booty on the dance floor, opera-loving Nick is unimpressed and flirty Michelle lets her hands do the talking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the second day of the competition in Cardiff and Nick is planning to bring some refinement and fine dining to the week's proceedings. However, fastidious Nick really has set himself a mountain to climb with a massive tasting menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third day in Cardiff, retail manager Yuno hopes to scoop the prize by whisking his guests off to the Himalayas. As well as entertaining his guests with some traditional dancing, Yuno's also hoping to tickle their taste buds by serving up Nepalese food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day four of the competition in Cardiff and turn of self-appointed 'hostess with the mostest' Michelle. She's out to impress with a culinary trip around the world, but when she sets a fun challenge, she ends up being accused of cheating

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night of the competition in Cardiff, host Suzanne is pinning her hopes on an evening of sophistication and fine French cuisine. Suzanne's menu is inspired by her many holidays to France - in fact she's been every year for the last 17 years. However, revealing the inspiration behind her starter might be a step too far for her guests, especially after the revelations found inside her 'special' cupboard!

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Art Bank in Dalbeattie, Dumfries and Galloway, where Mandy and Suzanne hope artistic flair will win them the week - but a shared bathroom is an instant black mark

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to the Tithe Barn Hotel in Cockermouth, Cumbria where hosts Amy and Anna hope their host-less hosting won't put off their guests but the self-check-in causes problems for one duo. Mandy and Suzanne dig up some issues from their own feedback during a visit to a mining museum, before the journey to the off-site breakfast the next morning proves hard to swallow for some B&Bers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On day three, married couple Dave and Meru hope their guests will love their traditional seaside guest house, The Sanderling in Morecambe. A walk along the Morecambe coast surprises some of the B&Bers, and a well-received bargain breakfast gives the hosts reason to be cheerful, but will some less-than-spotless cleanliness scores in the feedback tarnish their chances?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final visit is to the Curious Fox in Skipton, North Yorkshire, where hosts Lee and Janette think their combination of rooms and fine dining will wow their fellow competitors. However, an unwelcome discovery in one room could scupper them

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Toilet troubles, bathroom bothers and some very picky comments are all on the agenda before the payments are disclosed and it is revealed who has won

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three patients and how they cope when life takes an unexpected turn, including a patient who severed three of his fingers with an electric saw. Plus, a man with motor neurone disease is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis, and a teenager struggles to come to terms with the impact of serious injuries he sustained in a car crash six weeks ago

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An elderly woman is admitted with two severe leg fractures, with staff concerned she may not survive her injuries. Viktoria, her daughter, talks about Iris's life during the Blitz, and the touching story of her parents' romance that ended in tragedy. Elijah appears with an injured finger that he caught in a kebab slicer and 54-year-old Alison is rushed to A&E with sepsis, a life-threatening immune response to a chest infection

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 13-year-old aspiring Olympian fractures his pelvis after falling from his bike, an injury that may have devastating long-term consequences for his sporting career. As doctors determine if an operation is needed, his mother reflects on how a spinal injury brought an end to her own dreams when she was a teenager. A motorcyclist is brought in to St Georges, having been flung six feet in the air following a collision with a car

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Birmingham, paramedics Adi Ahmed and Mark McDonagh rush to a 10-year-old boy who has had a major epileptic fit. At the same time in Coventry, an elderly woman with heart and lung failure is struggling to breathe but refuses to go to hospital for treatment. Plus, in Warwick, a crew is called to an elderly man who has extreme anxiety and has called emergency services 14 times

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three patients and how they cope when life takes an unexpected turn, including a patient who severed three of his fingers with an electric saw. Plus, a man with motor neurone disease is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis, and a teenager struggles to come to terms with the impact of serious injuries he sustained in a car crash six weeks ago

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An elderly woman is admitted with two severe leg fractures, with staff concerned she may not survive her injuries. Viktoria, her daughter, talks about Iris's life during the Blitz, and the touching story of her parents' romance that ended in tragedy. Elijah appears with an injured finger that he caught in a kebab slicer and 54-year-old Alison is rushed to A&E with sepsis, a life-threatening immune response to a chest infection

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Kenya to see how cold-brew coffee differs from iced coffee. Helen Lawal tries frozen pastries in Portugal to see how well they perform well against fresh, while Briony May Williams heads to Staffordshire to learn how parsnips are cultivated to taste so sweet

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This competition takes place in and around Leeds, where the first to host is 24-year-old digital account manager Alice. Alice has never held a dinner party before, but she's hoping her family favourites will hit the spot and win her the £1,000 prize. She's hoping to impress Danielle, Andy, Lynne and Matt. With gob-smacking revelations, cringe-worthy party tricks and a ginormous Pavlova, can Alice manage to walk away with the honours?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Night two of the competition in Leeds, and company director Andy hopes to win with a menu of restaurant-inspired fare, but Alice and Danielle are more impressed with his Ferrari

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fun-loving student Danielle hosts the third dinner party in Leeds, hoping a high-calorie menu will see her emerge victorious. She even serves dessert wearing her wedding dress

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre technician Matt is the fourth to host in Leeds with a menu of family favourites - and if that does not win them over, hopefully his juggling skills will do the trick

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition in and around Leeds, and jeweller Lynne hopes her night will sparkle with a menu consisting of her own creations. While talk at the table turns to plastic surgery and Botox, Andy and Danielle aren't too impressed with Lynne's food

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Danni Menzies arrives in the beautiful coastal town of Nerja in southern Spain to meet house-hunter Frances, who is dreaming of buying a holiday home she can enjoy with her friends and family. With her daughter Karina also on hand to lend her support, Frances would love to find a two-bed property close to the sea, so Danni sets out to deliver her dream within a budget of £200,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Neil are about to start their next big adventure and move from their home in Exeter to start a new life in Lecce in southern Italy. They want a property that has a guest annex to accommodate friends and family, and that's close to the beach so Neil can surf whenever he wants. Armed with a budget of £200,000, Jasmine Harman shows them five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps social worker Gilly and her husband Andy to find their dream home where they can permanently relocate. Andy proposed to Gilly in Portugal 26 years ago, and now the couple have decided that the countryside inland from the Silver Coast would be the perfect place to spend their retirement. With Andy unable to join the search, Gilly brings along her daughter Kat for support. They're looking for a property with four bedrooms, a garden and a pool for their budget of £210,000. Ben amasses five options, but will any of them hit the right note and result in an offer?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George visits Manchester to bring an arts and crafts-inspired Edwardian home into the modern day. In London, he meets Flo, a first-time buyer whose flat needs a rejig to make it the sociable space she has always wanted

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the Payne-Durkins, on the rural outskirts of Leeds, to help Karl and Paul decide whether to renovate or sell their three-bedroom home. Karl loves the unique design and idyllic location, but Paul thinks they are too isolated, and would rather move to another area than spend the moving improving their current home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Birmingham, paramedics Adi Ahmed and Mark McDonagh rush to a 10-year-old boy who has had a major epileptic fit. programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps semi-retired builder Martyn and prison manager Sharon find their dream holiday home on Portugal's Algarve. Danni shows them five options, but will they love any of them enough to make an offer?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

Trish's dream is to find her perfect two-bedroomed property where she can holiday a couple of times a year with family and friends, and most importantly, enjoy the island's warm winters. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the second day of the competition in Cardiff and Nick is planning to bring some refinement and fine dining to the week's proceedings. However, fastidious Nick really has set himself a mountain to climb with a massive tasting menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third day in Cardiff, retail manager Yuno hopes to scoop the prize by whisking his guests off to the Himalayas. As well as entertaining his guests with some traditional dancing, Yuno's also hoping to tickle their taste buds by serving up Nepalese food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day four of the competition in Cardiff and turn of self-appointed 'hostess with the mostest' Michelle. She's out to impress with a culinary trip around the world, but when she sets a fun challenge, she ends up being accused of cheating

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night of the competition in Cardiff, host Suzanne is pinning her hopes on an evening of sophistication and fine French cuisine. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Art Bank in Dalbeattie, Dumfries and Galloway, where Mandy and Suzanne hope artistic flair will win them the week - but a shared bathroom is an instant black mark

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Britain's Railway Empire in Colour
programme.desc: To mark the 200th anniversary of the birth of rail, this series reveals the story of how Britain gave railways to the world - from the first tentative experiments with steam power, through the domestic boom years, to the exporting of this transformative new technology to all corners of the globe. Experts and enthusiasts and extensive newly colourised archive explore the truly revolutionary nature - both good and bad - of the locomotive. The first episode tracks how the railways grew from coal trucks in the north-east of England to transforming the lives of the Victorians. Trainspotter Francis Bourgeois tells the story of his 19th-century equivalent, schoolboy George Tod, who took stunning black and white photographs of his favourite engines. These and other photographs have been colourised, bringing the old railways back to life. There are also stories from the Empire as well as narratives from closer to home, including the magnificent Forth Bridge

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: OceanXplorers
programme.desc: vTitanic director James Cameron narrates and produces a six-part series, which travels around the world with ocean exploration initiative OceanX aboard its state-of-the-art vessel, the OceanXplorer, to uncover secrets of underwater worlds. Many of these ecosystems have never been glimpsed by human eyes and requires a handpicked team of experts and scientists to dive for up to 72 hours in the ship's two submersibles, Nadir and Neptune. This team comprises award-winning deep sea researcher Zoleka Filander, shark biologist Melissa Cristina Marquez, ocean technology Innovator Eric Stackpole and former Royal Marine Aldo Kane. Over the course of six episodes, the OceanXplorer and its crew plot a course from the Atlantic in the Azores through the warm waters of the Caribbean to the arctic shores of Svalbard, Norway

programme.desc: Documentary exploring extraordinary predatory shark behaviour, which seems to reverse conventional thinking on what sharks eat, and where. programme.title: Plunderer: The Life and Times of a Nazi Art Thief
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jonathan Petropoulos investigates the life of former Nazi art dealer Bruno Lohse, who became Hermann Göring's personal collector, tasked with finding desirable works of art. After the Second World War, Lohse spent a brief time in prison but later resumed his career as a dealer, often trading in art looted during the conflict

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: For his final tour, Paul Murton goes over the sea to Skye, where he visits the home of writer and naturalist Gavin Maxwell, before scaling the famous Cuillin mountain, Am Basteir. On the nearby Isle of Raasay he learns the amazing true story of the crofter Calum Macleod, who between 1964 and 1974 singlehandedly built a road to connect his isolated community

programme.title: Women Under Hitler's Flag
programme.desc: German women may be perceived as passive witnesses to the horrific crimes committed by the Nazi regime. However, as this documentary reveals many were active participants that were as brutal and merciless as their better-known, male counterparts. The first of two episodes begins with Hitler's arrival in power in 1933 and goes back to the roots of the indoctrination of young German girls within the female branch of the Hitler Youth (BDM)

programme.title: Women Under Hitler's Flag
programme.desc: Part two of two. The German women who were active participants that were as brutal and merciless as their better-known, male counterparts. In 1942, a new phase of the genocide took place - the 'Final Solution'. Man women, far from being mere witnesses or accomplices, played a role as important as it was deadly

programme.title: France: The Post War Recovery
programme.desc: Part one of two. The summer of 1944 marked the end of dark times for France. Aspiring to a better future, its people rolled up their sleeves and set out about rebuilding the nation's industry and agriculture. However, in post Second World War France, hopes and reality soon collided

programme.title: France: The Post War Recovery
programme.desc: Part two of two. From the Liberation of Paris to the Évian Accords signing Algeria's independence, via major social crisis of 1947, Abbé Pierre's appeal against poverty, the rise of modern comfort or the OAS attacks, this documentary paints a vast picture of a country torn between unity and division in the wake of the Second World War

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: For his final tour, Paul Murton goes over the sea to Skye, where he visits the home of writer and naturalist Gavin Maxwell, before scaling the famous Cuillin mountain, Am Basteir. On the nearby Isle of Raasay he learns the amazing true story of the crofter Calum Macleod, who between 1964 and 1974 singlehandedly built a road to connect his isolated community

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn relive the golden age of steam. They begin by exploring how the introduction of steam railways in the early 19th century created a domestic revolution, from the way people lived to the food they ate. Arriving at Ffestiniog Railway in Snowdonia, they find out how millions of tons of slate were moved down the mountain, while underground, Alex experiences the brutal conditions face by miners in the Llechwedd Quarry

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn continue to relive the golden age of steam and how the Victorian railways created modern Britain. They visit Beamish in County Durham, learning how the rail companies first realised there was money to be made in moving people rather than commodities. Then Peter and Alex live the life of navvies, the men who built the thousands of miles of tracks in all weather conditions, and Ruth finds out what impact the new railroads had on the cottage industries. She also learns about the role of the guard, and the trio learn what precautions were necessary for taking a long journey on a train that was yet to include the necessary facilities

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians reveal how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn discover how the mass transportation of livestock gave birth to the traditional British roast, and Ruth Goodman is in Whitby, which became a thriving fishing town thanks to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway supplying its kippers. Plus, Hampshire's watercress line, which brought nutritional salad to the masses, and how the use of steam-powered engines revolutionised production at Britain's oldest brewery

programme.title: Women Under Hitler's Flag
programme.desc: German women may be perceived as passive witnesses to the horrific crimes committed by the Nazi regime. However, as this documentary reveals many were active participants that were as brutal and merciless as their better-known, male counterparts. programme.title: Plunderer: The Life and Times of a Nazi Art Thief
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jonathan Petropoulos investigates Nazi art dealer Bruno Lohse's life in post-war America. Dealing in looted art, he became an invisible hand, even as the original owners' families sought the return of works rightfully theirs. Featuring an archive interviews with Lohse himself, as well as curators, investigators and descendants of the victims of Nazi art theft

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.sub-title: Life at the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: Paul Murton returns to visit more islands off the coastline, beginning with the Outer Hebrides. He resumes his journey on Eriskay, where he discovers how the remote location helped preserve a unique way of life for centuries - and also allowed locals to hide thousands of bottles of whisky from the excise men when a ship laden with the thirst-quenching cargo ran aground in 1941. The story inspired the 1949 movie Whisky Galore! Moving on to Barra, Paul goes foraging for cockles and visits the only airport in the world where scheduled flights land on the beach, then heads across to Vatersay, where he joins local fishermen trawling for lobsters

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Charting the pivotal battles of the American Civil War, underscoring their impact on the broader conflict and on American history. Beginning by examining the strategic decision made by Union and Confederate leaders and the intense combat experienced by soldiers at the battles of Shiloh and Antietam. Part one of three

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Highlighting the strategic decisions of Ulysses S. Grant and Robert E. Lee and the intense combat that defined the crucial battles of Vicksburg and Gettysburg, underscoring their impact on the broader conflict and American history. Part two of three

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Showcasing the intense fighting and strategic errors in the Battle of Chickamauga that led to significant losses on both sides. Plus, a look at General Grant's Overland Campaign, a series of battles aimed at defeating General Lee's forces. Part three of three

programme.title: The Civil War's Lost Massacre
programme.desc: The search for remains of formerly enslaved Black Union soldiers brutally murdered by angry Southerners as the Civil War was ending. Plus, a deep dive into Camp Nelson, Kentucky, the military base where these soldiers and thousands of others got their freedom papers

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.sub-title: Life at the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: Paul Murton returns to visit more islands off the coastline, beginning with the Outer Hebrides. He resumes his journey on Eriskay, where he discovers how the remote location helped preserve a unique way of life for centuries - and also allowed locals to hide thousands of bottles of whisky from the excise men when a ship laden with the thirst-quenching cargo ran aground in 1941. The story inspired the 1949 movie Whisky Galore! Moving on to Barra, Paul goes foraging for cockles and visits the only airport in the world where scheduled flights land on the beach, then heads across to Vatersay, where he joins local fishermen trawling for lobsters

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians take a trip on the Flying Scotsman, arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, along its original route connecting London and Edinburgh - the two most important financial capitals of the Empire. Peter Ginn explores how the railways revolutionised the delivery of mail across Britain, he and Alex Langlands learn about the first electronic communication service - more than 100 years before the arrival of email - and Ruth finds out about the role of the wheeltapper. Plus, why do some railway clocks have more than one minute hand?

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team discover how the combination of increased leisure time and affordable rail transport brought a new kind of freedom for working-class Victorians. Ruth Goodman travels along the south Devon coast from Paignton to Kingswear, where she helps prepare a paddle steamer for a journey up the River Dart, while at Swanage, Peter Ginn finds out what it was like to work on the excursion trains and the impact mass tourism had on the area

programme.title: Constantine the Great
programme.desc: The first in a two-part profile of the ancient Roman emperor, as archaeologists plot the different stages of his life from Naissus to Rome

programme.title: Constantine the Great
programme.desc: The concluding episode follows the emperor's archaeological traces to the new Rome, Constantinople, where his claim to power knows no bounds

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Charting the pivotal battles of the American Civil War, underscoring their impact on the broader conflict and on American history. programme.title: Cheating Hitler: Surviving the Holocaust
programme.desc: Three Canadian Holocaust survivors journey to home towns, killing sites, archives and hiding places in search of clues to unanswered questions from their pasts. Maxwell wonders what happened to a baby he saved in a forest in 1943, Helen wants to know more about the fate of her brother, while Rose is retracing the final steps of the woman who saved her life. The survivors came of age during the Holocaust and carry the burden of knowing they are the last living link to it

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the Isle of Mull and its satellite islands to discover why they have become boltholes from the hustle and bustle of the modern world. He begins on the tidal island of Erraid, which inspired famous Scottish writer Robert Louis Stevenson, then hops over to Ulva, the final resting place of Lachlan MacQuarrie, one of the first governors of New South Wales. He then explores Mull itself, being given a whistlestop tour of the island by rally driver Louise Thomas, then travels to the mysterious little island of Inch Kenneth, where he hears the story of Nazi sympathizer Unity Mitford. He ends his tour at the remote Treshnish Islands, where he experiences a spot of 'puffin therapy'

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead

Ruth Goodman travels along the south Devon coast from Paignton to Kingswear, where she helps prepare a paddle steamer for a journey up the River Dart, while at Swanage, Peter Ginn finds out what it was like to work on the excursion trains and the impact mass tourism had on the area

programme.title: Alexandria: The Greatest City
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes examines the history of the ancient city, which was founded by Alexander the Great in 331 BC. Until its decline in the fourth and fifth centuries AD, it became the centre of the world of art and culture and an example of advanced civilisation

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Perfect Husband

programme.title: Agony
programme.desc: Val decides to make a blue movie, after being advised by her horoscope for the week that there's a money-making scheme connected with the colour blue awaiting her

programme.title: Two's Company

programme.title: Two's Company
programme.desc: Dorothy asks Robert to help reunite a young married couple whose honeymoon happiness has already fallen apart.

programme.title: Mother's Ruin
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Roy Barraclough as a bachelor living with Dora Bryan as his belligerent mother.

programme.title: The Fenn Street Gang
programme.desc: The gang get a new member - Bernard Hedges.

programme.title: The Fenn Street Gang
programme.desc: Dennis thinks he knows who truly supports him but doubts creep in. Everything builds to a turning point when Maureen brings him along to a party.

programme.title: Frederick Forsyth Presents

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Figgis eavesdrops on a conversation between members of the hospital staff, and promptly passes on the gossip to all and sundry. James Bolam stars

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Glover hides from a vengeful Greek by having his head wrapped in bandages. Hospital comedy, starring Peter Bowles

programme.title: Carrott Del Sol
programme.desc: One-off comedy, starring Jasper Carrott as Sago, a likely lad who goes on a package holiday to Spain with two friends, Wayne and Kevin. After losing their money in a backgammon game, he enters them in a talent contest in a bid to win their money back

programme.title: The Man Who Made Husbands Jealous
programme.desc: Ferdie is so impressed by Lysander's success at reconciling Larry and Marigold that he decides to employ his services to patch up Guy and Georgie's rocky relationship. Meanwhile, Rannaldini's closeness to Flora doesn't go unnoticed. Adaptation of Jilly Cooper's racy novel, starring Stephen Billington, Gilly Coman, Kim Criswell and Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: The Man Who Made Husbands Jealous
programme.desc: Kitty and Lysander gradually fall for each other, but there's a major obstacle in the path of true love - her callous husband Rannaldini, who reacts to news of her pregnancy by suggesting she has an abortion. Adaptation of Jilly Cooper's novel, starring Stephen Billington, Kate Byers, Derek de Lint and Gilly Coman.

programme.title: Sex and the Other Woman (1972)
programme.desc: Anthology of five comic tales of married men having affairs. A henpecked husband risks getting caught in the act, an accountant's wife discovers her husband is sleeping with a colleague, a scheming seductress tries to break up her lover's marriage, a man has a relationship with his daughter's schoolfriend, and a deceived wife gets a tip-off from a nosy neighbour. Comedy, starring Bartlett Mullins and Maggie Wright

programme.title: Secrets of Sex (1970)
programme.desc: Erotic comedy, exploring the eternal battle of the sexes in a psychedelic mix of cosmic thrills and swinging permissiveness. Starring Richard Schulman and Janet Spearman

programme.title: A Clock Work Blue (1972)
programme.desc: A bumbling researcher is hurled through time, stumbling into the sensual secrets and erotic escapades that shaped civilization's wildest moments. Erotic comedy drama, starring Tracy Handfuss

programme.desc: Dr Hans Zarkov, Prince Barin, and Happy Hapgood rescue the wounded Flash and the leader of the Forest People orders the Earth people to be killed on sight.

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: Col. Rodriguez arrests an American journalist as a scapegoat for his nation's troubles. His wife learns of a letter that could expose Rodriguez. John Drake must recover it and bluff to win freedom!

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: While escorting two assassins to the United States, John Drake is forced down on a desert island. An old hermit, misled by the criminals, believes Drake and a stewardess are the real danger.

programme.title: Jack Hargreaves' Old Country
programme.desc: Jack celebrates rural England, blending folklore, crafts, and gentle storytelling as he explores vanishing traditions, landscapes, and the quiet wisdom of country life.

programme.title: The Lovers
programme.desc: Geoffrey tries to persuade Beryl into a trial marriage while her mother is away, but she stays one step ahead by arranging a chaperone, keeping the situation under control.

programme.desc: Dorothy and Robert wage a noisy feud with a neighbour over Robert's booming Bach records, ending up in court.

programme.title: The Russia House (1990)
programme.desc: British security services discover a book containing state secrets has changed hands at a Russian book conference. In order to discover if the information contained is genuine, they recruit a hard-drinking publisher to investigate where the documents came from - but while there, he embarks on a dangerous affair with a local woman. Thriller, starring Sean Connery, Michelle Pfeiffer, Roy Scheider, James Fox, John Mahoney and JT Walsh

programme.title: Wild Conamara
programme.desc: Part two of two. Eoin Warner heads inland on the second stage of his wild journey and finds one of the last remaining pockets of Atlantic Rainforest in Conamara - Derryclare woods. The woods are home to pine martens, red squirrels, and the newly arrived woodpecker. Crossing vast boglands, Eoin goes in search of lost birds, before heading to the highest of the 12 Bens, Binn Bhán

programme.desc: A criminal mastermind hopes to seal his reputation as the greatest villain of them all by stealing the Moon. Animated comedy, with the voices of Steve Carell, Jason Segel, Russell Brand and Julie Andrews

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and a team of volunteers renovate the home of 13-year-old Franek, who is severely disabled and relies entirely on his family for his care. programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Angel Has Fallen (2019)

Action thriller sequel, starring Gerard Butler and Morgan Freeman

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is feeling down in the dumps. The house is a mess, she can't seem to get any sleep and - to make things worse - Maria's home remedies end in disaster. Will the family show her some much-needed and long overdue appreciation? Cmedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: May 1945, and things look very different at Skeldale House as James is struggling with a wild and chaotic Siegfried. When Tristan returns from war and is shocked to see the shift in his brother, he and James decide to take action. But there's also some good news around the corner for everyone

programme.title: Keys to My Life
programme.desc: Country star Sandy Kelly revisits her nomadic childhood on the road with her family's Travelling Theatre Show in County Sligo. She also takes a trip to the castle that was the backdrop to her life-changing collaboration with country legend Johnny Cash. In London, Sandy explains how an opportunity to play Patsy Cline in a West End musical saved her from bankruptcy

programme.title: Fair City
programme.desc: Nikki is shocked when she discovers who betrayed her, Melanie tries to hide her true feelings for Mondo, while Orla annoys Sash when she rejects a voluntary job in prison. Rafferty turns down Tommy's request for help

programme.title: The Museum of Me

programme.title: The Sunday Game
programme.desc: Des Cahill introduces highlights and studio discussion of the best of the weekend's GAA action

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Young couple Sean and Jess O'Tuama from County Limerick are restoring a parochial house in rural County Limerick to its former glory. They plan to do some major structural repairs, tear apart the inside, reveal the original stonework and design their own kitchen and bedrooms. When the couple start peeling back layers of old plasterwork they realise that they have more work on their hands than they envisaged

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team is called out to deal with the case of an estate agent who has been viciously bludgeoned to death with a claw hammer. programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Alan Carr aims to transform the medieval tower room into a movie theatre inspired by Cinema Paradiso, while co-renovator Amanda Holden tries to turn the damp-ridden cellar carved out of the mountain's rockface into a wine grotto. Hitting a brick wall with their ideas, Alan takes Amanda to the Guicciardini Strozzi Casona winery to sample their world-famous beverages. Later, Alan gets to work creating a bespoke seating area, giving the entrance hall a much-needed makeover. Amanda builds a path to access the cellar, but discovers the rocky floor acts as a drain for water overflowing from the mountain

programme.title: Amanda & Alan's Italian Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr are on the home stretch as they renovate the kitchen by repurposing the old chestnut beds from Amanda's bedroom to form its units. Keen to source local Carrara marble for the worktops, the duo visit an active quarry in the Apuan Alps - but face a treacherous journey to get there. Later, with the work completed, they reveal their restored 17th-century house to their Tuscan friends and host an emotional farewell party

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Dorran, who has taken on the ambitious task of building a super-modern underground home on a hill outside Canterbury. He entrusts the work to a team of contractors, but when delays and rising costs force him to take charge of the build himself, Dorran faces the biggest challenge of his life, juggling his time between the site and his work as a ship's master out on the North Sea. However, having promised the house to his wife and children, he knows that failure is not an option

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Zimbabwean-born Davi and Matt from Australia, who are determined to create a home to reflect their backgrounds, so set out to build a house in the Chess Valley with a radical multicultural design, which is especially daring for their commuter-belt suburb. Despite their inexperience and the technical difficulty of the steeply sloping site, Davi and Matt manage the venture themselves. But it's not long before the problems begin

With many matches kicking off overnight, the duo do the late-night watching so viewers don't have to, delivering their trademark Cork humour and chaos alongside a morning roundup of all the latest action and talking points from the tournament

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Qatar v Switzerland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Tony O'Donoghue presents coverage of the Group B match at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in Santa Clara, USA. Qatar have earned outright qualification for a World Cup for the first time in their history, having made their only previous appearance at the tournament when they served as hosts four years ago. They'll hope to improve on that showing, when they lost all three of their group games against Netherlands, Senegal and Ecuador, scoring just one goal against the Senegalese in the process. The Swiss reached their sixth consecutive finals and 13th overall by topping a qualification group that contained Kosovo, Slovenia and Sweden, winning four and drawing two of their matches, while scoring 14 goals and conceding just two. There has only ever been one previous meeting between these nations, and Qatar will hope to replicate the outcome of that friendly in 2018, in which they claimed a 1-0 victory

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Brazil v Morocco (Kick-off 11.00pm). Tony O'Donoghue presents coverage of the Group C match at New York/New Jersey Stadium in the USA. Brazil are perennial competitors at the World Cup, having appeared in all 22 previous stagings of the tournament, while their five victories in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002 are more than any other nation has achieved. However, they are far from the dominant force they once were and finished fifth in the CONMEBOL qualification with a record of eight wins, four draws and six losses from their 18 games. In contrast, Morocco finished with an unblemished record in their qualifying campaign, winning all eight matches, while scoring 22 goals and conceding just two. They come into this tournament on the back of a controversial appearance in the AFCON final just five months ago, when they lost 1-0 to Senegal in extra time after Senegal left the pitch and refused to return after conceding a penalty in the final minute of regulation time, which Morocco's Brahim Diaz then missed when the game eventually resumed. However, three months later the CAF Appeal Board ruled that Senegal had forfeited the final through their actions, awarding Morocco a 3-0 victory, subject to a ruling from the Court of Arbitration for Sport

programme.title: Shortscreen
programme.desc: Coming-of-age story about the emotional tug-of-war experienced by a young man, Conor, from an economically deprived part of post-industrial North East England

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Ivory Coast v Ecuador (Kick-off 12.00am). Coverage of the Group E match at Philadelphia Stadium in the USA. This will be the first-ever meeting between these nations, and both will be aiming to get their campaigns off to a winning start having performed admirably in qualifying for this tournament. Les Éléphants finished atop their group in the CAF region after finishing with a record of eight wins and two draws from their 10 matches, while La Tricolor finished second in the highly competitive CONMEBOL region, trailing only Argentina, even after they were deducted three points related to an infringement from a previous World Cup qualification cycle. Neither of these nations has a stellar World Cup record from previous appearances, with Ivory Coast never having progressed beyond the group stage, and not having qualified since 2014, while Ecuador have appeared at four World Cup finals, most recently four years ago, making it to the last-16 on just one occasion

programme.title: The Sunday Game Live
programme.desc: Tyrone v Mayo (Throw-in 3.30pm). Joanne Cantwell presents coverage of the round 2A match at O'Neills Healy Park, Omagh. Both teams won away in the previous round, with Tyrone eliminating Roscommon and Mayo prevailing against Monaghan. With analysis from Brian Fenton

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Germany v Curaçao (Kick-off 6.00pm). Peter Collins presents coverage of the Group E match at Houston Stadium in the USA. The Caribbean nation face one of football's most decorated nations in their first-ever World Cup finals match as they go up against the four-time champions, who have also reached the final on four other occasions. Since their most recent World Cup victory at the 2014 tournament in Brazil, Die Mannschaft have failed to progress from the group stage and have only won two matches at the last two tournaments combined. Julian Nagelsmann's men came through qualifying with a record of five wins and one loss so will be confident of performing better this time around, but will be wary of an opponent they know little about having never faced them before, but who went undefeated in two stages of qualifying in the CONCACAF region

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Netherlands v Japan (Kick-off 9.00pm). Peter Collins presents coverage of the Group F match at Dallas Stadium in the USA. There have been three previous meetings between these nations, including a group-stage match at the 2010 World Cup in South Africa when a second-half goal from Wesley Sneijder was enough to give the Dutch a 1-0 victory. Both teams went on to progress to the knockout stage, and while the Japanese would bow out in the last-16, Oranje made it all the way to the final but fell short of victory in a 1-0 loss to Spain. Both teams were present at the previous World Cup in Qatar, and both topped their respective groups, with the Dutch leading Group A ahead of Senegal, Ecuador, and the host nation, while Samurai Blue bested Spain, Germany and Costa Rica. The most recent clash between tonight's opponents was a friendly match in 2013 that took place in Belgium, which saw Japan salvage a 2-2 draw after being 2-0 down

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: USA v Paraguay (Kick-off 2.00am). Clare MacNamara presents coverage of the Group D match at Los Angeles Stadium in Inglewood. While the Americans were guaranteed a place in this tournament as one of the three co-hosts, Paraguay progressed from the CONMEBOL qualification process, and though they finished sixth in the standings, they were one of four teams to finish tied on 28 points and were just one point behind second-placed Ecuador. There have been nine previous meetings between these nations, the first of which saw the USA claim a 3-0 victory at the inaugural World Cup in Uruguay in 1930, and most recently they clashed in a friendly encounter in November last year, when the United States triumphed by a 2-1 scoreline

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Haiti v Scotland (Kick-off 2.00am). Clare MacNamara presents coverage of the Group C match at Boston Stadium in the USA. Scotland are appearing at a World Cup finals for the first time since France '98, and their eighth overall, having finished top of a qualifying group that contained Denmark, Greece and Belarus. This will be their first-ever meeting with Haiti, who had to come through two phases of qualification to reach the finals for the second time in their history, with the previous appearance being in 1974, in West Germany. They will hope to perform better than they did on that occasion, when they lost three from three and conceded 14 goals, so will welcome an opener against an opponent that has only won four World Cup finals matches in their history, the most recent of which was a 2-1 win over Sweden at Italia '90

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Australia v Türkiye (Kick-off 5.00am). Coverage of the Group D match at BC Place Vancouver in Canada. The Socceroos are appearing at their sixth consecutive finals, and a seventh overall, and will hope to build on their experience in Qatar four years ago when they progressed to the knockout stage for just the second time. They faced European opposition twice in the group stage on that occasion and had mixed fortunes, beginning their campaign with a 4-1 loss to eventual runners-up France, before beating Denmark 1-0 in their final group match, which helped them to move on to a meeting with eventual winners Argentina. In contrast, Türkiye have only appeared in World Cup finals on two previous occasions, most recently in 2002 when the competition had co-hosts for the first time in Japan and South Korea, and they will hope to perform just as admirably as they did on that occasion when they reached the semi-finals and were beaten 1-0 by eventual champions Brazil

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Sweden v Tunisia (Kick-off 3.00am). Coverage of the Group F match at Monterrey Stadium in Mexico. Sweden have a fairly disappointing record of qualifying for the World Cup in recent times, only appearing three times since they finished third at USA '94, and they had to rely on the UEFA play-offs to secure their spot this time around. For Tunisia this is their third consecutive appearance at a World Cup finals and their seventh overall, and they'll hope to take advantage of the new expanded format and qualify for the knockout stage for the first time. They came up short in Qatar four years ago despite claiming a credible 0-0 draw with Denmark and memorable 1-0 victory over France, with a 1-0 loss to Australia ultimately seeing the Socceroos finish above them in Group D as they progressed to the last 16 alongside eventual runners-up, Les Bleus

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Bayliss and Pembleton investigate a terminally ill man's death which is not as natural as it first seems. Hard-hitting police drama, starring Kyle Secor and Andre Braugher

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: When a four-year-old boy disappears from a carousel, Falsone and Stivers catch the case and are joined by the entire homicide unit

programme.title: The Guest Wing
